title: Mutual Action Plan & final clearance checkpoint for Novastar POC
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena@novastar.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Doug Walters <doug.w@novastar.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T16:12:00-08:00
Subject: MAP & final approval timeline for Novastar POC

Hi Chloe / Camila,

Thanks again for the walkthrough earlier. Attached is the mutual action plan (Novastar_MAP_20261101.xlsx) and the latest SOW (Novastar_SOW_v3.pdf). We have an internal exec sync on Nov 10 but we are aiming to lock a final go/no-go by the week of Nov 9 so we can keep the proposed start date of Nov 17.

Can you confirm:
- Who will be the internal approvers at Redwood (legal, procurement, exec owner)?
- A realistic cutover window if we pass the MAP (preferred start = 2026-11-17)
- Whether you can route the SOW for Esign once legal confirms redlines

Doug and I will be available for a short decision meeting on Nov 9–10. If you can share a DocuSign flow and expected signature owners, that would speed things up.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Head of Platform Integrations
Novastar Retail

Attachments: Novastar_MAP_20261101.xlsx (worksheet), Novastar_SOW_v3.pdf (PDF)

From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T09:03:00-08:00
Subject: RE: MAP & final approval timeline for Novastar POC

Team — forwarding Novastar MAP/SOW. High level proposal for internal owners and dates:

- Chloe Martin (Sales): coordinate exec sign-off, target complete by 2026-11-04
- Camila Reyes (Customer Success / Onboarding): confirm readiness checklist and pre-prod window by 2026-11-06
- Ben Carter (Legal): review SOW and return redlines by 2026-11-06
- Kimberly Park (Procurement): confirm PO and payment terms by 2026-11-09

Planned customer-facing checkpoint: 2026-11-09 10:00 PT — internal decision + brief with Elena/Doug for go/no-go. If all green, we route DocuSign and lock start date Nov 17.

Please reply with any conflicts or changes to owners. Ben — if you need additional context from Novastar, say so and I’ll loop them in.

-Chloe

--- Original attachments retained ---

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-03T11:18:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Novastar SOW review - initial redlines

I reviewed Novastar_SOW_v3.pdf and summarized the material redlines. Quick notes:

- Liability cap: recommend capping at 1x ARR for POC; customer requested 3x — will try to reach middle ground 1.5x
- Data retention & deletion: tighten language around KV cache persistence; add 30-day data purge after termination for POC data sets
- Service credits: clarify latency SLO definition for evaluation period (exclude planned maintenance)

If these are acceptable as negotiation points, I can produce SOW_redlines_v1.pdf (attached) and flag the clauses for DocuSign placeholders. With minor edits I can sign off on or before 2026-11-08. If we see heavier legal pushback from Novastar we should expect +2 business days.

--Ben Carter
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachments: SOW_redlines_v1.pdf

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-05T14:27:00-08:00
Subject: RE: MAP status and pre-prod checks

Quick update from CS / evaluations:

- MAP execution: feature parity test passed for 90% of test cases. Remaining: long-tail prompt routing (low priority).
- Performance: scheduled final latency & throughput run for 2026-11-09 08:00–09:30 PT against a production-like shard. I will upload results (latency_report_20261109.csv) immediately after.
- Onboarding readiness: our kickoff checklist expects the first 48 hours of dedicated support; confirm that Legal/PO timeline aligns so we can staff Nov 17.

If legal or procurement needs additional artifacts (data flow diagrams, retention screenshots), I can pull them and send to Elena's team.

—Camila

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-06T10:45:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Procurement path and PO timing

Procurement update: Novastar has confirmed intent to purchase pending legal and exec approvals. Our side:

- PO issuance target: 2026-11-10 (Novastar finance has a payment window they prefer)
- Payment terms: Net 30 as standard; if Novastar requests Net 45 we can accept but need to reflect in the deal economics
- DocuSign: we need a final pricing summary and signature order (who signs first? legal or CFO?) to create the envelope.

Camila / Chloe — please confirm final MRR/one-time onboarding fees to include on the DocuSign cover sheet. Once Ben confirms redlines and we have the HubSpot deal ID I will request the PO template.

-Kimberly

From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@novastar.com>, Doug Walters <doug.w@novastar.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-11T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Final clearance checkpoint and DocuSign plan (Novastar POC, DEAL-78921)

Elena / Doug — pulling everything together for the checkpoint next week. Summary and ask: 

1) Internal owners (Redwood): Chloe (exec approval), Ben (legal redlines), Kimberly (procurement/PO), Camila (CS kickoff readiness).
2) Customer checkpoint: 2026-11-09 10:00 PT — we will confirm go/no-go based on legal redlines and the latency run. If we get a go, Redwood will send a DocuSign envelope the same day with the SOW and signature order (Novastar CFO -> Novastar Head of Engineering -> Redwood CEO).
3) Target start date: 2026-11-17. This assumes PO issue by 2026-11-10 and no material legal rework.

Ben attached SOW_redlines_v1.pdf; Camila will add latency_report_20261109.csv after the run. Kimberly will assemble the pricing sheet for DocuSign. HubSpot deal link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/DEAL-78921

If Novastar is aligned, please confirm the signature order and whether their CFO is comfortable with Net 30 payment terms. Once confirmed, we will post the DocuSign powerform link and route the envelope.

Thanks — Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
chloe@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0211

title: MAP: evaluation gating & GPU pool onboarding — sequencing and acceptance checklist
preview:
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-28T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — capacity questions

Thanks, Ben — this is helpful. A couple clarifying questions before we lock dates:

1) Our procurement window for GPU commits is 8–10 weeks from PO. Can Redwood start capacity hold earlier if we sign a MOU?
2) We need to finalize staging slots for a 2-week acceptance window. Can you outline what pass/fail looks like for the MAP evaluation gate?

Attaching the internal procurement calendar.

—Priya
Director, Cloud Platform
Aurelia Health

---
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Aurelia Procurement <procure@aureliahealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-27T13:18:00-07:00
Subject: MAP kickoff — proposed sequencing & acceptance criteria

Priya — thanks for the time last week. Per our conversation I sketched a short MAP that sequences eval -> acceptance -> capacity reservation so we can align procurement deadlines with onboarding. High level:

- Eval sprint (2 weeks): functional smoke tests, latency & cost baseline, tenant isolation test
- Staging acceptance (2 weeks): throughput at target qps, cold-start SLA, KV cache warmup run
- Capacity hold & procurement handoff: 8–10 week lead from PO to tranches delivered; we suggest a 30% buffer on initial pool size to account for early spike.

Actions for Aurelia: confirm target p95 latency, provide sample traffic profile and core compliance doc list.
Actions for Redwood: deliver eval image, reserved staging slots, and a draft SOW with tranche schedule.

I've attached a draft MAP workbook and the initial SOW. Let me know if you want a quick call to walk through.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0122

Attachments: Aurelia-MAP-v2.xlsx; SOW-draft-Aurelia.pdf

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-04T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — capacity questions

Ben / Karthik — looping in the specific staging plan here. Notes from platform:

- Lead time: Our Dedicated pools are typically provisioned in 6–9 weeks for tranche 1 (subject to region). Private/on-prem will vary — 10–14 weeks due to hardware shipping and on-site validation.
- Minimum pool sizing guidance: for their stated QPS (~120 qps steady, 350 qps bursts), recommend starting with 8 x A100-equivalent slots using medium batching profiles; plan for a second tranche within 60 days if inference quality/regression tests are satisfied.
- Acceptance gating: pass criteria = 95th percentile latency <= target p95 from contract, successful end-to-end KV-cache warmup with no >1% error rate, and scripted throughput test sustaining target QPS for 30 minutes.

I'll draft a short annex that can be dropped into SOW to make these gating criteria contractual.

—Marissa
Senior Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

---
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Aurelia Procurement <procure@aureliahealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-05T11:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — capacity questions

Marissa — thanks, that level of specificity helps. A few follow-ups:

- The procurement team needs a formal commitment window to include in the PO. Is Redwood comfortable with a non-binding 45-day capacity hold followed by a firm PO to lock tranches?
- For Private deploys, we need a checklist of on-site acceptance tasks (network config, KMS validation, audit log retention). Can you attach that?

Procurement copied here — we expect to convene on 11/12 to align internal approvals.

Best,
Priya

---
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-11T15:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP kickoff — capacity questions

Priya — quick note from infra/procurement side: we can accommodate a 45-day non-binding hold (we label it a 'soft hold' in the MAP workbook) that guarantees compute tranches are reserved for Aurelia while they finalize PO. Key constraints:

- Soft hold requires a 20% refundable deposit to cover commodity pre-reservation costs if hardware is scarce (standard for large tranches).
- If they elect Private/on-prem, the 45-day hold kicks off Kitting and vendor lead-time coordination; final PO still required 30 days before scheduled ship to avoid re-schedule fees.
- I'll attach the on-site checklist and a compressed capacity CSV with the suggested tranche breakdown (first tranche: 8 A100-eq; second tranche: 6 A100-eq).

Ben — recommend we convert the MAP workbook to a tracked SOW annex and include the soft-hold language and deposit terms. Priya / Procurement — if that matches your expectation, can you confirm by EOD 11/12 so Marissa and I can lock dates with ops?

Thanks,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Infrastructure Partnerships | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai

> Attachment: capacity-estimate-20261104.csv
> Attachment: private-deploy-checklist.pdf

title: Keynote starter credits + swag fulfillment
preview:
From: DevSummit Ops <ops@devsummit.org>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Cc: emma@devsummit.org
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: starter credits + swag codes for keynote speakers

Hi Stephanie,

We're finalizing the logistics for the keynote speaker welcome packs and have a last-minute request from the programming team. Can Redwood provide:

- 50 starter promo codes (API trial credits) to hand out to keynote speakers
- 30 single-use swag redemption codes (to be printed on welcome cards)

Event date: Nov 30. We need codes generated and a simple CSV delivered by Nov 20 so our print shop can include the cards. Also, any paperwork required (T&Cs) would be helpful.

Let me know if there's a cost or if you need an internal PO.

Thanks,
Emma Li
DevSummit Operations
ops@devsummit.org

Attachment: keynote_speaker_list.csv (names + shipping addresses)


From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: starter credits + swag codes for keynote speakers

Looping you in — DevSummit needs 50 starter promo codes and 30 swag redemption codes; deadline to deliver CSV is Nov 20.

Marissa: do we have budget in the events/promo pool to cover 50 30-day trial grants?
Ben: can infra generate single-use codes with 90-day expiration and a one-click redemption URL?
Vanessa: will ops handle shipping or should we pass to our fulfillment vendor?

I don't want to block the printer; if we can approve a limited pool and hand over a CSV that should be fine. If this needs procurement/PO, flag it.

— Stephanie
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
stephanie@redwood.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: starter credits + swag codes for keynote speakers

Stephanie — good catch. Marketing can approve 50 starter promo codes from the event promotional budget (Q4 campaign pool).

Details/ask from finance:
- Codes should be time-limited: recommend 90-day expiration to give speakers time to try Redwoods features.
- Make them single-use and tag them in the system as "devsummit-2026-keynote" for tracking.
- Cost estimate: roughly equivalent to ~$1.50 per starter grant (expected usage), so budget impact ~ $75 plus minor admin overhead.

Please attach the DevSummit speaker list and I'll sign off. Also attaching promo policy for compliance.

Attachment: promo_policy.pdf

Marissa Cole
Sr. Growth Ops, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: DevSummit Ops <ops@devsummit.org>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 14:23:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Re: Request: starter credits + swag codes for keynote speakers

Forwarding to our fulfillment partner + ops.

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: Codes & shipping manifest for DevSummit

Stephanie — per Marissa's approval I've generated 50 single-use starter promo codes (90-day expiry) and created a shipping manifest for the swag batch. Attached: event_codes.csv and shipping_manifest.xlsx. Codes are tagged "devsummit-2026-keynote" and include a one-click redemption URL column.

Ben Carter

title: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@holohealth.com>, Miguel Torres <miguel@holohealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Sara / Miguel,

Thanks again for the time yesterday. I attached a first-pass Mutual Action Plan (HoloHealth_MAP_v1.xlsx) that lays out a 6‑week evaluation and pilot cadence. The key decision we want to surface in week 2 is deployment mode and region (Hosted API in us-east vs Dedicated reserved pool vs Private/VPC or on‑prem).

High-level asks for you so we can finalize the MAP:
- Priority ranking: latency SLA, data residency, encryption/KMS, throughput.
- Do you have a strict data residency requirement for PHI storage (US‑east / specific state)?
- Procurement timeline / PO lead times?

Quick notes on tradeoffs:
- Hosted (fastest onboarding, can pin region to us‑east, 48–72h test access)
- Dedicated (predictable throughput, stronger isolation; typical provisioning 2–4 weeks depending on capacity)
- Private (VPC/on‑prem, best for regulated PHI controls; 8–16 week delivery and security review)

Can you confirm the top two priorities and any hard constraints by EOD next Tuesday (Nov 2)? I’ll coordinate internal owners after that.

Attachments: HoloHealth_MAP_v1.xlsx, SOC2_Attestation.pdf (for procurement)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

---
[Attachment stubs: HoloHealth_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), SOC2_Attestation.pdf (pdf)]

Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Ben — thanks, this is helpful. Quick answers:
- Priority 1: Data residency + compliance (must remain in US; prefer us‑east).
- Priority 2: SLA for latency and uptime — our clinical workflows need consistent sub‑250ms inference for summary/triage flows.
- Procurement: expect a 3–4 week procurement review; procurement will request SOC2 and an initial security questionnaire.

Can you confirm whether Dedicated gives us the stronger SLA we need, and whether Hosted can meet the latency target if pinned to us‑east? Procurement will want SOC2 paperwork (you included that — thank you).

Best,

Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 08:43:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Team — forwarding HoloHealth's priorities (data residency US / prefer us‑east; latency <250ms SLA; procurement 3–4 weeks). They asked whether Dedicated is required to meet SLA or if Hosted pinned to us‑east is sufficient.

Requests:
- Kimberly: ballpark monthly delta (cost & lead time) between Hosted (region pinned) and Dedicated reserved pool to meet sustained ~15k inference requests/day with 95th percentile sub‑250ms. Include any quantization / batching levers that would change cost estimate.
- Vivek: are there pre‑reqs for KMS/HSM or contractual language procurement will insist on if we propose Dedicated? Also flag Private / on‑prem timelines and security gating.
- Marissa: suggested MAP milestone edits — what internal owners should we assign for security evidence and pilot health checks?

Need replies by EOD Nov 6 so I can send an updated MAP back to HoloHealth and propose the pilot commitment link.

Thanks,
Ben

[Forwarded message excerpted above]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 10:23:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Ben / team — summary below and attaching a short estimate (dedicated_capacity_estimate.pdf).

Summary:
- Hosted (us‑east pinned): Likely meets median latency targets; 95th percentile depends on burst patterns. For sustained 15k/day with even traffic distribution, we expect 95th ~220–300ms depending on model size & batching.
- Dedicated: predictable 95th percentile <250ms with properly sized GPU pool. Typical provisioning 10–18 business days (depends on queue / capacity availability). Monthly committed cost delta vs hosted is ~1.8x–2.2x for the capacity required; can reduce by model quantization & concurrency tuning.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): strongest isolation and compliance; delivery 8–16 weeks and additional integration work (KMS/HSM work + network review).

Recommendations for MAP:
1) Offer a 30‑day Dedicated pilot (discounted) in us‑east to validate 95th under their production-like traffic. Pilot triggers: dataset drop, baseline eval set, and production‑like concurrency.
2) If procurement needs lower procurement risk, propose Hosted with region pin + an SLA addendum for the pilot window while we provision Dedicated.
3) Prepare SOC2 packet + simple KMS integration checklist for procurement.

Attachment: dedicated_capacity_estimate.pdf (capacity footprint and ballpark pricing)

Kimberly Park
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com

---
[Attachment stub: dedicated_capacity_estimate.pdf]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Nov 2026 09:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Ben / team — quick procurement & security notes to fold into the MAP: 
- If HoloHealth requires KMS/HSM-backed key control or on‑prem connectors, Security needs to run the standard intake; Private path estimate is 12–16 weeks with security sign‑off.
- For Dedicated we can support customer KMS integrations (bring-your-own-key) but that adds ~2 weeks to provisioning for key exchange and paperwork.
- Suggest we offer a temporary Dedicated pilot with a 30‑day credit and an opt‑out clause in the MAP if SLA targets are not met — lowers procurement friction.

Also flag: include an itemized acceptance test (latency p95/p99 targets on their eval set) as part of the MAP so we have a concrete close criterion.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference
vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com

Date: Tue, 11 Nov 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Evaluation playbook + hosting choice — HoloHealth MAP draft

Sara / Miguel — thanks for the clear priorities and thanks to Procurement in advance for reviewing the SOC2 packet. Based on internal checks we recommend the following MAP update and next steps:

1) Week 0–1: Confirm priorities + sign pilot commitment (DocuSign link below).
2) Week 1–2: Provision Dedicated pilot in us‑east (discounted 30‑day pilot) while Hosted region‑pinned fallback remains available.
3) Week 2–4: Run production‑like eval set; acceptance criteria: p95 < 250ms and throughput steady for 48h.
4) Week 4–6: Procurement / contract negotiation; if Dedicated meets acceptance criteria, convert pilot to reserved Dedicated capacity. If not, decide on Hosted with SLA addendum or move to Private path (longer runway).

Attachments: SOC2_Attestation.pdf (for procurement), dedicated_capacity_estimate.pdf, HoloHealth_MAP_v1.xlsx (updated).

Please review and, if the plan looks good, sign the pilot commitment here: https://www.docusign.com/rdw/RDW-4982-pilot-commit (link above). I’m happy to jump on a quick call Thursday to walk through the acceptance test and provisioning plan.

Next internal owners: Kimberly (pilot provisioning & sizing), Vivek (security & KMS questions), Marissa (success criteria & eval scripts).

Thanks — looking forward to moving this to pilot.

Best,
Ben Carter

title: Customer key escrow exception request for private edge handoff
preview:
From: Sam Torres <sam.torres@medisafe.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Legal Intake <legal@medisafe.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 14:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: Exception to key escrow for on-premedge deployment

Hi Ava / Legal team,

Quick note following our technical workshop last week — Medisafe is moving forward with a private-edge deployment that will sit in our hospital partner data centers. The deployment model requires that the customer holds the master key (BYOK) and that Redwood has no ability to decrypt production data. Our security team understands Redwood's standard escrow/escrow-operator model, however we need an exception to the keystore escrow clause in the DPA for this specific deployment.

Main asks:
- Can Redwood confirm a formal exception path to avoid key escrow for on-prem / customer-controlled KMS?
- What operational controls would replace escrow to give us assurance (audit logs, operator access windows, breakglass auditability)?
- Any subprocessors that would need access to the deployed environment and their controls?

We'd like a written summary we can share with our compliance board by Friday. I've copied Priya (legal) and Legal Intake for intake.

Thanks,
Sam
Sam Torres
Sr. Security Program Manager, Medisafe Health

Attachment: Medisafe_DPA_Annex_A.pdf (attachment stub)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Sam Torres <sam.torres@medisafe.com>
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 16:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Exception to key escrow for on-premedge deployment

Sam —

Thanks for the clear summary. We're supportive of a model where the customer retains the master CMK in their HSM and Redwood operates against a wrapped/delegated key in the private deployment. To address your specific asks at a high level:

1) Exception path:
- We can include a DPA addendum that permits an escrow exception for deployments that meet predefined technical gates (isolated VPC, no cross-tenant networking, customer-managed KMS with attested HSM).
- The addendum would include explicit operator access constraints (time-boxed maintenance windows, jump-host logging, and MFA-brokered sessions).

2) Operational controls proposed to replace escrow:
- Continuous operator action logging shipped to a customer-owned SIEM (Syslog/CloudWatch endpoint) and immutable audit archives retained for 1 year.
- Breakglass process: emergency access only via OPA-reviewed request with two-person approval and egress captured to an independent audit bucket.
- Proof-of-control evidence: weekly tamper-evident snapshots and signed attestations for the first 90 days post-handoff.

3) Subprocessors & access:
- For on-prem deployments we generally limit subprocessors: our telemetry/observability vendor will process anonymized telemetry; our managed-ops partner (if engaged) signs an OTO addendum restricting key access.
- We'll provide a subprocessor list and per-subprocessor control matrix in the SOW appendix.

Next steps I'd propose: Redwood drafts the DPA addendum + SOW appendix and a 1‑page operational controls summary for your compliance board. Dev can sketch the technical gating criteria and Marcus will produce an operator access runbook excerpt.

I'll start the draft today — copying Dev and Marcus to capture operational details. Is Friday acceptable for the compliance summary?

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachment: SOW-custkey-exception-draft.docx (attachment stub)


From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Sam Torres <sam.torres@medisafe.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Exception to key escrow for on-premedge deployment

Folks —

Quick technical notes to feed into the draft:
- Gate criteria for escrow exception (minimum): isolated VPC with explicit ACLs, no egress to Redwood-controlled networks except our logging endpoint, customer CMK in FIPS-140 cert HSM with attestation chain, and operator sessions constrained to bastion with recorded session logs.
- Cryptography flow: Redwood will use a wrapped key (per-tenant ephemeral DEK) generated at deploy time; the master CMK never leaves customer HSM. Our runtime will call a local KMS proxy; any operator actions that would normally request CMK unwrap must be performed by the customer or via a customer-approved breakglass where the unwrap audit is recorded.
- Telemetry: we will only collect anonymized service metrics and explicit operational logs; PII/payloads will not leave the customer environment.

I'll attach a short ops excerpt in the Drive link for the SOW appendix. Marcus — please confirm the runbook excerpt (operator checklist + maintenance window template) and Evelyn, can Compliance/Legal coordinate the DPA addendum wording?

-Dev

--Forwarded message--
From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>
To: internal-ops@redwood.com
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:22:05 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: Exception to key escrow for on-premedge deployment

(Ops excerpt in Drive link)


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <priya.rao@medisafe.com>, Legal Intake <legal@medisafe.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 09:03:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Request: Exception to key escrow for on-premedge deployment

Priya / Medisafe Legal —

Thanks for the patience while we pulled the details together. We've drafted a focused DPA addendum and an SOW appendix that enumerate: escrow exception criteria, operator access controls, required attestations, and the subprocessor matrix for this deployment. Highlights:
- No escrow: master CMK remains in customer HSM; Redwood will not hold or escrow the key.
- Operator actions that may require key unwrap are limited to customer-approved maintenance windows and require 2‑party approval; all actions produce signed audit entries.
- Subprocessors: ObservabilityCo (anonymized telemetry only) and ManagedOpsPartner (no key access; signed operational constraints).
- Evidence: we can provide weekly signed audit bundles and a one-time attestation on handoff.

I've attached the DPA addendum draft link in the HubSpot/Drive items and a proposed timeline for signatures. If the board needs an executive summary, Ava — feel free to use the one-pager I drafted in the SOW link.

Next: Priya, can you review the legal language and mark any redlines. If we get alignment today we can lock the SOW and route contracts to signature and schedule the initial handoff runbook session.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Head of Privacy & Security Ops, Redwood Inference

Attachment: DPA_Addendum_Draft.pdf (Drive link in related_links)

- Data retention: default 30 days for marketplace logs; customers may request longer with enterprise contract.

4) Ops and rollout risks:
- If Integratech's webhook delivery retries are >24 hours, we need a queued fallback at Redwood to avoid missing attribution events.
- SSO metadata exchange: provide cert and ACS URL to our onboarding tool.

title: Zenotech - audit log retention partitioning & sample export plan
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>, Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the RFP submission earlier this week. A couple of follow-ups from our compliance team: we need clarity on how Redwood partitions audit logs by tenant and activity class (admin events vs. content events), how retention windows are enforced per partition, and how exports (full vs. delta) are delivered.

Specifically: 1) Can you provide a sample export (<=10k lines) showing manifest format, per-record metadata (tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp), and tamper-evidence hashes? 2) Can retention be tiered per partition (30d/180d/3yr) and enforced automatically with verifiable deletion timestamps? 3) What SLA and timeline do you recommend for bulk export (30TB) vs. scheduled incremental exports?

If possible, please attach a sample manifest and a technical note on partitioning. We are on a tight timeline for our audit next month.

Regards,
Liam
GRC Lead, Zenotech Financial



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
Cc: Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>, Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Liam —

Thanks — we can do all three items. I attached a short export manifest (CSV) and a technical note that outlines our partitioning model and retention enforcement. High level summary below:

- Partitioning: logs are sharded by tenant_id and event_class. Each partition has its own immutable append-only ledger and manifest.
- Retention policy: per-partition retention policies are configured via the Console or API (e.g., 30d, 180d, 3yr). Deletions are performed via immutable tombstones and recorded in a deletion ledger that captures deletion_time, operator_id, and a verifiable hash of the pre-deletion manifest.
- Export types: full export (snapshot) and delta export (since-seq-id). Exports include a manifest with per-record metadata and SHA-256 item hashes for tamper evidence.
- SLAs/timelines: for a 30TB bulk export we typically quote 7–10 business days to prepare, validate, and stage an encrypted S3 transfer; incremental scheduled exports (hourly/daily) are available with nearline delivery (minutes to hours) depending on requested chunk size and routing.

I'll loop in engineering for the sample and a clear timeline. In the meantime, attaching: redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf and sample-export-manifest.csv.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Ben/Marissa — forwarding Zenotech's follow-up. They want a <=10k-line sample manifest and a technical note on per-partition deletion evidence plus an estimated timeline for a 30TB bulk export.

Ask: can engineering produce a geared sample that includes tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp, item_hash, and an attached CSV they can consume? Also need a short paragraph describing how deletions are recorded (deletion ledger + tombstone hash).

Goal: send the sample + a concise SLA/timeline back by end of day Monday 11/9 so they have it for their audit prep.

Thanks —
Aisha



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T13:40:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Aisha —

We can produce the sample manifest and a short technical blurb. Notes from infra:
- We'll generate a synthetic 10k-line CSV with the exact schema Zenotech requested. Each line will include tenant_id, event_class (admin|auth|content|system), sequence_id, iso_timestamp, payload_length, sha256(item), and a partition_digest for the batch.
- Deletion flow: when retention policy expires we atomically write a tombstone record to the deletion ledger (tombstone_id, deletion_time, policy_id, operator_id) and include the pre-delete partition_manifest_hash. These logs are immutable and exported as part of compliance bundles.
- Bulk export cadence and sizing: for 30TB we propose a three-stage plan — prepare (validate manifests, encryption keys) 2-3 days, stage & transfer (multi-part encrypted S3 upload) 3-6 days depending on egress bandwidth, final verification 1 day. Recommend 7 business days baseline; accelerate to 4 with priority engineering and additional egress capacity (cost adder).

I'll drop the synthetic CSV and a chain-of-custody template for their legal team.

Ben
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
Cc: Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>, Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>

Subject: RE: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Liam / team —

Attached are the items requested for your compliance review:

1) sample-export-manifest.csv — 9,872 synthetic lines (schema described below).
2) redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf — short note on partitioning and deletion ledger semantics.
3) chain_of_custody_template.docx — template we use for evidence handoffs.

Quick schema summary (also included in the CSV header): tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, iso_timestamp, payload_type, payload_length, sha256_item_hash, partition_batch_id, partition_digest, export_manifest_id. The manifest includes a top-level partition_manifest_hash and a signing signature using our HSM-backed key.

Retention & deletion evidence (summary):
- Per-partition retention is configurable by policy and enforced by automated tombstone issuance.
- Tombstone records are appended to a deletion ledger which itself is exported with every archive; each ledger file includes pre-delete manifest_hash and a cryptographic digest chain to detect tampering.
- We can provide an audited manifest and sample deletion ledger excerpt for any given tenant range upon request.

Export timelines:
- Delta (since-seq) exports: typically minutes–hours depending on window and size; ideal for nearline ingestion.
- Bulk 30TB export: baseline 7 business days (prepare + stage + transfer + verify). Priority path: 4 business days with additional network egress and an expedited engineering run. Cost/ops estimate included in the PDF.

Next steps: please confirm whether Zenotech would like a prioritized 4-day run (we will need written confirmation and a procurement PO to allocate dedicated egress). If not, we will plan for the 7-business-day standard run.

Thanks and happy to jump on a quick call if you prefer to review the manifests together.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf, sample-export-manifest.csv, chain_of_custody_template.docx
text:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>, Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T09:15:00-08:00
Subject: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Aisha,

Thanks for the RFP submission earlier this week. A couple of follow-ups from our compliance team: we need clarity on how Redwood partitions audit logs by tenant and activity class (admin events vs. content events), how retention windows are enforced per partition, and how exports (full vs. delta) are delivered.

Specifically: 1) Can you provide a sample export (<=10k lines) showing manifest format, per-record metadata (tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp), and tamper-evidence hashes? 2) Can retention be tiered per partition (30d/180d/3yr) and enforced automatically with verifiable deletion timestamps? 3) What SLA and timeline do you recommend for bulk export (30TB) vs. scheduled incremental exports?

If possible, please attach a sample manifest and a technical note on partitioning. We are on a tight timeline for our audit next month.

Regards,
Liam
GRC Lead, Zenotech Financial



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@zenotech.com>
Cc: Zenotech Procurement <procurement@zenotech.com>, Avery Singh <avery.singh@zenotech.com>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: RFP follow-up: retention partitioning and on-demand samples

Hi Liam —

Thanks — we can do all three items. I attached a short export manifest (CSV) and a technical note that outlines our partitioning model and retention enforcement. High level summary below:

- Partitioning: logs are sharded by tenant_id and event_class. Each partition has its own immutable append-only ledger and manifest.
- Retention policy: per-partition retention policies are configured via the Console or API (e.g., 30d, 180d, 3yr). Deletions are performed via immutable tombstones and recorded in a deletion ledger that captures deletion_time, operator_id, and a verifiable hash of the pre-deletion manifest.
- Export types: full export (snapshot) and delta export (since-seq-id). Exports include a manifest with per-record metadata and SHA-256 item hashes for tamper evidence.
- SLAs/timelines: for a 30TB bulk export we typically quote 7–10 business days to prepare, validate, and stage an encrypted S3 transfer; incremental scheduled exports (hourly/daily) are available with nearline delivery (minutes to hours) depending on requested chunk size and routing.

I'll loop in engineering for the sample and a clear timeline. In the meantime, attaching: redwood-partitioned-retention-specs.pdf and sample-export-manifest.csv.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>

Date: 2026-11-07T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Ben/Marissa — forwarding Zenotech's follow-up. They want a <=10k-line sample manifest and a technical note on per-partition deletion evidence plus an estimated timeline for a 30TB bulk export.

Ask: can engineering produce a geared sample that includes tenant_id, event_class, sequence_id, timestamp, item_hash, and an attached CSV they can consume? Also need a short paragraph describing how deletions are recorded (deletion ledger + tombstone hash).

Goal: send the sample + a concise SLA/timeline back by end of day Monday 11/9 so they have it for their audit prep.

Thanks —
Aisha



From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T13:40:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Zenotech - retention partitioning & sample export plan (coordination)

Aisha —

We can produce the sample manifest and a short technical blurb. Notes from infra:
- We'll generate a synthetic 10k-line CSV with the exact schema Zenotech requested. Each line will include tenant_id, event_class (admin|auth|content|system), sequence_id, iso_timestamp, payload_length, sha256(item), and a partition_digest for the batch.
- Deletion flow: when retention policy expires we atomically write a tombstone record to the deletion ledger (tombstone_id, deletion_time, policy_id, operator_id) and include the pre-delete partition_manifest_hash. These logs are immutable and exported as part of compliance bundles.
- Bulk export cadence and sizing: for 30TB we propose a three-stage plan — prepare (validate manifests, encryption keys) 2-3 days, stage & transfer (multi-part encrypted S3 upload) 3-6 days depending on egress bandwidth, final verification 1 day. Recommend 7 business days baseline; accelerate to 4 with priority engineering and additional egress capacity (cost adder).

I'll drop the synthetic CSV and a chain-of-custody template for their legal team.

Ben

title: Service advisory: elevated 503 pattern impacting hosted generation endpoints
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Hi Priyanka,

I wanted to let you know we’re currently seeing an elevated pattern of 503 responses on hosted generation endpoints (primarily POST /v1/generate). Your traffic from Clarion.ai appears to be affected in the US region this morning — we’re prioritizing impact for live production calls and we’ve opened an incident to track this.

What we know so far:
- Start time (observed): 2026-11-10 08:05 PST
- Symptom: intermittent 503 responses, bursty errors, increased p95 latency for generation requests
- Scope: subset of hosted capacity in us-west; SDK and REST clients both impacted

What we’re doing:
- Engineering is on an incident bridge now and looking at autoscaling/controller events and recent deploys.
- We’ve added a temporary routing rule to reduce load on affected pools for high-priority customers.

Immediate ask: are you seeing a specific API endpoint or route that’s most critical for you? If so, reply with an example request ID and we’ll target that flow.

We’ll post live updates to the incident page: https://status.redwood.com/incidents/RED-20261110-1 and the bridge link below.

Thanks for the patience — I know this hurts traffic for you. I’ll follow up within 30 minutes with an update or earlier if we have a mitigation.

Bridge: https://join.redwood.com/bridge/RED-20261110-1
Attachment: initial-error-graph.png (PNG, 74 KB) — the first minute of error-rate by backend pool.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:18:30 -0800
Subject: Re: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Alex —

Thanks for the heads up. We’re seeing failures in our live inference path (user-facing generation) and it’s causing request spikes and retries. Example failing request id: req_8f3a2b5d (timestamp 08:16 PST).

Can you confirm whether Clarion.ai customers are being routed to any fallback model or older instance groups? Also, we’ll need an ETA — this is affecting a portion of our paying users and product is tracking errors to the team.

If you can provide a short update to our SRE and PM (copied) it’d help us manage the incident internally.

—Priyanka
Priyanka Rao
Head of Platform @ Clarion.ai
priya@clarion.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 08:25:05 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Re: Notice: elevated 503 pattern on hosted generation endpoints (investigating)

Team — forwarding Priyanka’s note. Quick thoughts:
- We should proactively offer a temporary credit for impacted production calls if this looks multi-hour.
- Ops/Eng ask: can we throttle non-customer background traffic in affected pools to preserve live traffic? If yes, coordinate on the bridge.
- Comms: Alex, please include language around ETA and whether Dedicated/fallback routing is available. PR should be factual and concise.

I’ll be ready to help run customer comms once we have confirmed mitigation steps.

-Ben
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Clarion Support <support@clarion.ai>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:02:40 -0800
Subject: Status update: mitigation in progress (RED-20261110-1)

Priyanka — quick update from the bridge.

Summary of current status:
- Root cause (preliminary): an autoscaler controller regression in our routing plane that incorrectly trimmed warm backends during a scaling event; this produced short-lived capacity gaps and a cascade of 503s for affected pools.
- Scope: hosted generation pools in us-west only; we are not seeing similar patterns in eu or apac at this time.
- Impact: intermittent 503s and elevated p95 for generation calls between 08:05–08:55 PST; error rate reduced substantially after a mitigation at ~08:58 PST.

Mitigations applied:
- Reverted the autoscaler config change and forced a slow rewarm of removed instances.
- Temporarily pinned traffic from Clarion.ai to a higher-priority serving pool and bypassed one failing controller.
- Read-only metric collection retained for postmortem; we’ve attached the most relevant logs.

Next ETA:
- We expect to have full stable capacity restored and verification completed by 10:00 PST. We will keep the pinned routing until verification passes a 30‑minute stability window.

Attachments: clarion-logs-20261110.txt (text, 130 KB) — redacted request ids and backend traces.

We understand the customer impact and Ben is coordinating a credits/comp package. We’ll send a short incident summary and a proposed credit offer by end of day.

If you want a synchronous sync, join the bridge: https://join.redwood.com/bridge/RED-20261110-1 (ops only link)

Thanks for the patience — we’ll follow with a post-incident write-up and proposed remediation timeline.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference



From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Priyanka Rao <priya@clarion.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Follow-up: incident RED-20261110-1 — summary and next steps

Priyanka —

Final note from the incident today. Full timeline and summary below for your records.

Timeline (summary):
- 08:05 PST: elevated 503s begin (autoscaler trimming observed)
- 08:12 PST: customer notifications and bridge opened
- 08:25 PST: internal comms on credits/mitigation
- 08:58 PST: mitigation applied (revert + pinned routing)
- 09:02 PST: error rate reduced ~95%
- 09:40 PST: verification checks started; no recurrence seen
- 10:00 PST: capacity restored and monitoring window began

Root cause (final, preliminary): a controller regression pushed in the autoscaler staging pipeline that had insufficient guards for simultaneous drain + scale operations; during a rapid traffic shift, the controller marked warm nodes as removable and a gap in capacity caused 503 spikes. We have reverted the change and are hardening the guardrails.

Customer impact: Clarion.ai experienced increased client-side retries and ~2–5% of production requests returned 503 during the window. We estimate the bulk of errors occurred between 08:05–08:58 PST.

Actions we’re taking (next steps):
- Code fix: roll back the problematic controller change and add unit/integration tests to prevent similar trims (timeline: fix merged and staged, release planned within 48 hours).
- Platform changes: add an additional safe-check in the autoscaler to prevent simultaneous drain+scale during high request churn.
- Postmortem: detailed postmortem doc and RCA will be shared by 2026-11-12 EOD.
- Compensation: Ben will send a proposed credits package within 24 hours.

Attachments: postmortem-outline-draft.pdf (PDF, 85 KB) — preliminary postmortem outline and affected metrics.

We are sorry for the disruption. If you’d like a review call to go through the postmortem and remediation plan, please propose times and we’ll set one up.

Regards,
Alex Martinez
Customer Reliability Manager
Redwood Inference

On Tue, Nov 10, 2026 at 09:02 AM Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com> wrote:
> Priyanka — quick update from the bridge.
> 
> Summary of current status:...

title: Post-discovery: align ops playbook & book 60–90m ML infra deep dive
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Alice Monroe <alice@acmedata.com>, Bob Chen <bob.chen@edgeware.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 17:22:00 -0800
Subject: Post-discovery: align ops playbook & book 60–90m ML infra deep dive

Hi Alice / Bob —

Thanks again for the call today — really helpful to hear the architecture constraints and your timing goals. Quick recap and a proposal for next steps: 

- Recap: Acme is focused on predictable inference latency for batched workloads, wants stronger KV-cache behavior across sessions, and needs clear data residency and audit logging commitments for the pilot.
- Next: we recommend a 60–90 minute technical deep dive (ML infra + ops) with our infra & SRE leads to walk through the serving runtime, batching strategy, and a short Q&A including quantization/perf tradeoffs.

Can we book a 60–90m slot next week? Proposed windows (PST):
- Tue 2026-11-16 10:00–11:30 or 14:00–15:30
- Wed 2026-11-17 13:00–14:30 or 15:00–16:30

Please confirm which window works and who from your side should join (engineering, security, procurement). I’ve cc’d Ben (solutions) and Priyom (eng programs) — they’ll run the deep dive.

I’m attaching a short agenda draft and an ops preflight checklist to help prep. If you have any sample traffic/profile or a runbook excerpt you want us to review ahead of time, please share.

Warmly,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman | Senior AE, Redwood Inference
Attachments: acme-tdx-agenda-v1.pdf, acme-ops-checklist.xlsx


From: Alice Monroe <alice@acmedata.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Bob Chen <bob.chen@edgeware.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 20:05:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Post-discovery: align ops playbook & book 60–90m ML infra deep dive

Thanks, Aisha — this is perfect and matches what we discussed. A couple quick notes: 

- Availability (our team, EST): Tue 11/16 13:00–15:00 EST (which should map to your 10:00–12:00 PST), and Wed 11/17 16:00–17:30 EST (your 13:00–14:30 PST). Either of those windows works.
- Attendees: myself (Platform Lead), Bob Chen (ML infra), and I’ll loop in our security lead, Maria Lopez, if the agenda includes audit/logging and KMS.
- Prework: we’d like the session to cover data residency patterns, tokenization/cost implications for long context runs, and how caching affects multi-tenant isolation. Can your infra team add a short slide/runbook on expected latency variance under batching?

Also: can we do a 15-minute pre-call right before the deep dive to confirm which sample traces we’ll share? We prefer to share a redacted runbook snippet rather than full logs — I can attach that.

I’ve attached a short runbook excerpt and our security contact info.

Best,
Alice
Alice Monroe | Platform Lead, Acme Data Labs

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>

Subject: Post-discovery: align ops playbook & book 60–90m ML infra deep dive

Hi Alice / Bob —

Thanks again for the call today — really helpful to hear the architecture constraints and your timing goals. Quick recap and a proposal for next steps: 

- Recap: Acme is focused on predictable inference latency for batched workloads, wants stronger KV-cache behavior across sessions, and needs clear data residency and audit logging commitments for the pilot.
- Next: we recommend a 60–90 minute technical deep dive (ML infra + ops) with our infra & SRE leads to walk through the serving runtime, batching strategy, and a short Q&A including quantization/perf tradeoffs.

Can we book a 60–90m slot next week? Proposed windows (PST):
- Tue 2026-11-16 10:00–11:30 or 14:00–15:30
- Wed 2026-11-17 13:00–14:30 or 15:00–16:30

Please confirm which window works and who from your side should join (engineering, security, procurement). I’ve cc’d Ben (solutions) and Priyom (eng programs) — they’ll run the deep dive.

I’m attaching a short agenda draft and an ops preflight checklist to help prep. If you have any sample traffic/profile or a runbook excerpt you want us to review ahead of time, please share.

Warmly,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman | Senior AE, Redwood Inference
Attachments: acme-tdx-agenda-v1.pdf, acme-ops-checklist.xlsx

From: Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: payables@novacore.fr
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 09:14:00 +0000
Subject: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Hi Ava and Monica,

I hope you're both well. Quick heads up from Novacore: we completed an internal legal reorganization last month and are splitting our payment routing across two legal entities. Effective 2026-12-01, payments for US contracts should go to:

  - Novacore US, Inc. (remit: ACH bank ending 6782)
  - Novacore Global Solutions (for EU/APAC invoices; SEPA/Intl)

We are also moving our North American headquarters to 4100 Harborview Ave, Suite 300, Seattle, WA 98104 — the new legal address will appear on invoices starting 2026-12-01.

Attached: a bank letter on company letterhead and our updated onboarding form. We also included a signed W-9 for your records. Please let us know what you need from Redwood to update the vendor record and whether upcoming POs will require reissue to the new remit entity.

Attachments (stubs): Novacore_bank_letter.pdf [PDF 85KB], Novacore_signed_onboarding_form.pdf [PDF 220KB], W-9_Novacore.pdf [PDF 45KB]

Thanks,
Apolline Duval
Head of Finance Operations
Novacore Solutions
apolline.duval@novacore.fr
+33 6 12 34 56 78

Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 10:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Thanks for the update, Apolline — congratulations on the reorganization. A few quick items so we can process this cleanly on our end: 

  1) Can you confirm the legal entity that should appear on Redwood POs and invoices for existing contracts (Novacore US, Inc. vs Novacore Global Solutions)?
  2) Please confirm the effective date for payment routing (you mentioned 2026-12-01). Will any invoices dated before that continue to be paid to the current account?
  3) Finance (cc'd) requires a bank verification letter on bank letterhead AND a short confirmation from your treasury that the ACH routing is valid for vendor payables. If possible, include a contact for a bank operations person.
  4) Are there any tax withholding implications we should be aware of given the entity split?

We typically update vendor remittance in our AP system and run a verification ACH before switching. Monica, Marcus — can we put this through the vendor-change checklist (forms + bank letter + ACH test)? If everything looks good, we'll change remit instructions and update the remit address on active POs where necessary.

Appreciate the attachments — I'll loop back after finance verifies.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0124

title: Renewal capacity planning: Dataforge reserved pool scenarios
preview:
From: Elena Park <elena.park@dataforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hudson <thudson@dataforge.ai>, Renewals <renewals@dataforge.ai>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Capacity planning for upcoming renewal (Nov 30)

Hi Ben,

Our renewal for the Dedicated pool is coming up on Nov 30 and the finance team asked me to get a clearer capacity recommendation from Redwood before we sign. Quick background: we currently run a 8xA100 reserved pool with auto-burst to 12 for short windows. Typical steady-state traffic is ~2.5k QPS for the primary inference endpoint, with peak windows up to ~4.2k QPS for 90m/day. Average request is ~350 total tokens (prompt+response). We have a ~75% prefix cache hit on repeated prompts.

We're planning an aggressive product push in Q1 that the PMs estimate could increase normal QPS by 30-50% over the next 6 months. Our priorities are: predictable latency for our paid tier (99th pct token latency target: < 120ms), predictable unit cost for forecasting, and headroom for spikes without manual intervention.

Can you send a few sizing scenarios (conservative/expected/aggressive) with recommended reserved GPU counts, suggested burst capacity, and a ballpark uplift to monthly committed spend? If you need any more telemetry, I can export per-hour QPS and p95/p99 latency traces.

Thanks,
Elena
Elena Park
Head of Platform
Dataforge Labs


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@dataforge.ai>
Cc: Tom Hudson <thudson@dataforge.ai>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 12:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Capacity planning for upcoming renewal (Nov 30)

Hi Elena — thanks for the context, this is exactly what we need. A few clarifying Qs before I assemble formal scenarios:

- Which model variant are you serving (name + size)? Any custom fine-tuning or response-length policies that increase output tokens beyond the average you quoted?
- Current observed p50/p95 token latencies and whether the 75% cache hit is stable across endpoints or concentrated on a couple of routes?
- Do you have any contractual constraints around change windows for the Dedicated pool (e.g., cannot increase GPUs without 30d notice)?

I'll run your numbers through our capacity model and send conservative / expected / aggressive sizing with estimated monthly committed cost, suggested autoscale burst policy, and an optional quantization/packing scenario to reduce required GPUs. Attaching the capacity model spreadsheet (df_capacity_model_v3.xlsx) — if you can drop in your per-hour QPS and p99 traces I can run the simulation and return updated numbers.

Proposed timeline: I can get you a first pass by Thu EOD if you can share the trace CSVs by tomorrow.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Park <elena.park@dataforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Hudson <thudson@dataforge.ai>, Renewals <renewals@dataforge.ai>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 10:21:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Capacity planning for upcoming renewal (Nov 30)

Ben — thanks, attaching the CSV export (hourly_qps_202610.csv) to this thread. Answers inline:

- Model: "redwood/llm-instruct-v2-70b" (8-bit quantized in production). We do a 1.2x average response multiplier in some agents for longer follow-ups, but the 350 token average is the weighted mean across routes.
- Latencies (current dedicated pool): p50 token latency 18ms, p95 65ms, p99 115ms. These were measured over the last 30 days.
- No contractual change windows — we can discuss commitment windows but prefer a 12-month term with a scaling clause if possible.

Finance wants a clean topline uplift number for a proposed 12-month renewal with two options: (A) conservative reserved (minimal uplift) and (B) growth-oriented reserved (higher commit, lower unit cost).

—Elena

On Wed, Nov 3, 2026 at 09:12 Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena,
>
> Our renewal for the Dedicated pool is coming up on Nov 30...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:00:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Dataforge renewal — sizing input + customer constraints

Folks — forwarding Dataforge's traffic export and answers. They expect 30–50% QPS growth over next 6 months and want a 12-month commitment with a scaling clause. Customer target: p99 token latency < 120ms.

My first-pass recommendations (run using df_capacity_model_v3.xlsx, assumptions at bottom):

1) Conservative (min uplift) — Expected traffic +20% growth assumption
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,000
   - Recommended reserved pool: 10 x A100 (40GB)
   - Suggested burst: autoscale to 14 x A100 for 3x short windows (kept at 15m max each event)
   - Expected unit cost uplift vs current (8xA100): ~+28% monthly committed spend
   - Notes: Achieves p99 ~110ms with current 75% prefix cache assumption.

2) Expected (planning) — Expected traffic +35% growth
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,375
   - Recommended reserved pool: 14 x A100
   - Suggested burst: autoscale to 20 x A100 for sustained campaign days (allows 99th pct < 120ms with 10% headroom)
   - Expected uplift vs current: ~+60% monthly committed spend

3) Aggressive (growth-oriented) — Expected traffic +50% growth + product push spikes
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,750; peak windows ~5k QPS
   - Recommended reserved pool: 18 x A100 with immediate burst to 26 (mix of reserved + guaranteed burst pool)
   - Optional: convert to mixed H100-backed fleet for densification (12 x H100 equivalent), we modeled a 12 H100 reserved + 8 A100 burst which lowers average cost by ~12% but requires a custom routing policy and RBAC for preemptable capacity.

Pricing / term suggestions:
- 12-month commitment with quarterly checkpoints (month 1, month 4, month 8) to evaluate usage and optional uplift clauses.
- Include a documented autoscale burst pool (pay-for-burst or pre-committed burst credits) to prevent throttling during campaign spikes.
- If they sign an expected/aggressive tier, offer an initial onboarding credit (1 month pro-rated) and a 5% discount on unit compute for >15 GPU commit.

Attached: a one-page cost/throughput summary (dataforge_commitment_draft.pdf) and the capacity model I used. Please review the numbers (I rounded GPUs to whole units) and flag any kernel/runtime constraints we should call out.

Assumptions in model (high-level): average tokens 350, cache hit 75% prefix, model throughput on native A100 (fp16) ~180 QPS per A100 for this workload at 350 tokens; quantized pack gains push to ~240 QPS/A100. We used conservative latency degradation for QPS >95% of capacity.

Karthik — can you validate the per-GPU QPS assumptions and whether recommending mixed H100/A100 will add operational complexity we should price into the proposal? Marissa — can you draft a renewal summary with the two customer-facing options (Conservative / Expected) and simple one-line pricing so Elena's finance can route?

Thanks,
Ben


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@dataforge.ai>
Cc: Tom Hudson <thudson@dataforge.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Dataforge renewal — recommended commitment options and next steps

Elena — thanks again for the traces and the details. Based on the modeling and internal validation we propose two clean options for your 12-month renewal (summary below). I attached the draft commitment PDF with the line-item pricing table and a short appendix that maps estimated QPS to GPU counts.

Summary (topline):
- Option A — Conservative (lower uplift, low risk)
  - Reserve: 10 x A100
  - Burst: pre-approved autoscale to 14 x A100 (pay-as-you-burst for overflow)
  - Monthly committed compute uplift: ~+28% vs current
  - SLA: 99th pct token latency target < 120ms (monitored, with credit bands for sustained SLA breaches)

- Option B — Growth (lower unit price, more headroom)
  - Reserve: 14 x A100 (or 12 x H100 equivalent — see attached appendix for tradeoffs)
  - Burst: guaranteed burst pool to 20 x A100 or equivalent H100 mix
  - Monthly committed compute uplift: ~+60% vs current (unit price reductions applied for >15 GPU commit)
  - SLA: 99th pct token latency target < 120ms with prioritized routing and extra monitoring retention

Operational / contract notes:
- We recommend a 12-month term with quarterly usage checkpoints and a scaling clause that allows a single upward adjustment within the first 90 days without penalty.
- If Dataforge chooses Option B, we will include a one-time onboarding credit equivalent to 4 weeks of committed spend and add a migration plan to introduce H100 capacity for densification (if agreed).
- We will instrument p99 token latency alerts to feed into your SRE Slack channel and provide a monthly headroom report.

Next steps:
1) Please confirm whether you want the conservative vs growth option. If helpful, we can run one final simulation using your 7-day rolling peak to fine-tune the GPU counts.
2) If you approve Option B, we'll produce a formal contract amendment with a committed SKU list and draft DocuSign link for signature.

Link to draft commitment & appendix: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DfCapacityModelShared
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/DF-2026-RNWL-004

If you prefer, we can jump on a 30-min call this week to walk through the table live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0189

--
Redwood Inference — Production-grade inference for every team

To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:00:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Dataforge renewal — sizing input + customer constraints

Folks — forwarding Dataforge's traffic export and answers. They expect 30–50% QPS growth over next 6 months and want a 12-month commitment with a scaling clause. Customer target: p99 token latency < 120ms.

My first-pass recommendations (run using df_capacity_model_v3.xlsx, assumptions at bottom):

1) Conservative (min uplift) — Expected traffic +20% growth assumption
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,000
   - Recommended reserved pool: 10 x A100 (40GB)
   - Suggested burst: autoscale to 14 x A100 for 3x short windows (kept at 15m max each event)
   - Expected unit cost uplift vs current (8xA100): ~+28% monthly committed spend
   - Notes: Achieves p99 ~110ms with current 75% prefix cache assumption.

2) Expected (planning) — Expected traffic +35% growth
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,375
   - Recommended reserved pool: 14 x A100
   - Suggested burst: autoscale to 20 x A100 for sustained campaign days (allows 99th pct < 120ms with 10% headroom)
   - Expected uplift vs current: ~+60% monthly committed spend

3) Aggressive (growth-oriented) — Expected traffic +50% growth + product push spikes
   - Target steady-state QPS: ~3,750; peak windows ~5k QPS
   - Recommended reserved pool: 18 x A100 with immediate burst to 26 (mix of reserved + guaranteed burst pool)
   - Optional: convert to mixed H100-backed fleet for densification (12 x H100 equivalent), we modeled a 12 H100 reserved + 8 A100 burst which lowers average cost by ~12% but requires a custom routing policy and RBAC for preemptable capacity.

Pricing / term suggestions:
- 12-month commitment with quarterly checkpoints (month 1, month 4, month 8) to evaluate usage and optional uplift clauses.
- Include a documented autoscale burst pool (pay-for-burst or pre-committed burst credits) to prevent throttling during campaign spikes.
- If they sign an expected/aggressive tier, offer an initial onboarding credit (1 month pro-rated) and a 5% discount on unit compute for >15 GPU commit.

Attached: a one-page cost/throughput summary (dataforge_commitment_draft.pdf) and the capacity model I used. Please review the numbers (I rounded GPUs to whole units) and flag any kernel/runtime constraints we should call out.

Assumptions in model (high-level): average tokens 350, cache hit 75% prefix, model throughput on native A100 (fp16) ~180 QPS per A100 for this workload at 350 tokens; quantized pack gains push to ~240 QPS/A100. We used conservative latency degradation for QPS >95% of capacity.

Karthik — can you validate the per-GPU QPS assumptions and whether recommending mixed H100/A100 will add operational complexity we should price into the proposal? Marissa — can you draft a renewal summary with the two customer-facing options (Conservative / Expected) and simple one-line pricing so Elena's finance can route?

Thanks,
Ben


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@dataforge.ai>
Cc: Tom Hudson <thudson@dataforge.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 16:40:00 -0800
Subject: Dataforge renewal — recommended commitment options and next steps

Elena — thanks again for the traces and the details. Based on the modeling and internal validation we propose two clean options for your 12-month renewal (summary below). I attached the draft commitment PDF with the line-item pricing table and a short appendix that maps estimated QPS to GPU counts.

Summary (topline):
- Option A — Conservative (lower uplift, low risk)
  - Reserve: 10 x A100
  - Burst: pre-approved autoscale to 14 x A100 (pay-as-you-burst for overflow)
  - Monthly committed compute uplift: ~+28% vs current
  - SLA: 99th pct token latency target < 120ms (monitored, with credit bands for sustained SLA breaches)

- Option B — Growth (lower unit price, more headroom)
  - Reserve: 14 x A100 (or 12 x H100 equivalent — see attached appendix for tradeoffs)
  - Burst: guaranteed burst pool to 20 x A100 or equivalent H100 mix
  - Monthly committed compute uplift: ~+60% vs current (unit price reductions applied for >15 GPU commit)
  - SLA: 99th pct token latency target < 120ms with prioritized routing and extra monitoring retention

Operational / contract notes:
- We recommend a 12-month term with quarterly usage checkpoints and a scaling clause that allows a single upward adjustment within the first 90 days without penalty.
- If Dataforge chooses Option B, we will include a one-time onboarding credit equivalent to 4 weeks of committed spend and add a migration plan to introduce H100 capacity for densification (if agreed).
- We will instrument p99 token latency alerts to feed into your SRE Slack channel and provide a monthly headroom report.

Next steps:
1) Please confirm whether you want the conservative vs growth option. If helpful, we can run one final simulation using your 7-day rolling peak to fine-tune the GPU counts.
2) If you approve Option B, we'll produce a formal contract amendment with a committed SKU list and draft DocuSign link for signature.

Link to draft commitment & appendix: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DfCapacityModelShared
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/DF-2026-RNWL-004

If you prefer, we can jump on a 30-min call this week to walk through the table live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>

Subject: Post-call: PII handling & log sample request (data residency check)

Hi Laura / FinMedia Security team,

Thanks again for taking the time today — great discussion. Quick recap and agreed next steps so we stay aligned: 

- Ask from FinMedia: sample audit logs showing who accessed customer artifacts (transcripts, embeddings) + an export process and confirmation of EU-only residency for customer data.
- Deliverables Redwood agreed to provide:
  1) A 30-day redacted log sample (CSV + JSONL) with columns: timestamp, event_type, object_type, object_id (hashed), actor_id (pseudonymized), model, region, request_id, workspace_id.
  2) A short write-up of how PII is handled in logs (hashing/salting approach, retention controls).
  3) Confirmation of storage region and whether we can pin this customer's data to eu-west-1 for production.
  4) Export mechanics: approximate runtime, formats, and a stub process doc.

Attachments: postcall-minutes.pdf (short notes from today's demo).

Can you confirm the exact CSV columns you need and whether we should include hashed user identifiers vs. fully redacted (we proposed pseudonymized IDs)? Also, what time window should we prepare — you asked for 30 days in the call but confirm if you need more.

Proposed ETA: we can produce the 30-day redacted sample within 10 business days after engineering picks this up; Rishi from our security team will scope the redaction approach.

Next step: Laura, if you confirm columns and window (30 days vs 90 days), we'll spin up the export and share the stub sample and process doc.

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0193

On Mon, Nov 5, 2026 at 14:00 FinMedia Security <security@finmedia.com> wrote:
> Hi Neha, Laura here — joined from Security.
>
> Thanks for the clear recap. Two clarifications from our side so the sample is meaningful for our audit team:
>
> 1) Column requirements: We need a stable pseudonym for user identity (so we can correlate actions across days) and a separate column indicating whether the request payload included PII (yes/no). Please include the hashing algorithm you used for the pseudonyms.
> 2) Residency proof: We need a short statement confirming primary-at-rest location for the dataset backing the logs (EU - eu-west-1) and whether backups cross-region.
>
> We'd like the 30-day sample, but if producing that is operationally heavy for you, a 7-day representative sample is OK for an initial review.
>
> Laura
> Head of Security, FinMedia
> laura.chen@finmedia.com


From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@finmedia.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-06T09:08:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: PII handling & log sample request (data residency check)

Hi Neha — thanks. A couple more items from procurement/security that we'd like to include in the deliverable list: 

- Retention policy: what are default retention windows for raw request logs vs aggregated telemetry? Our compliance team expects at least 1 year of access logs, but we need to map that to your current defaults and any configurable options.
- Export format: CSV must be accompanied by a JSONL equivalent (line-delimited JSON) so our tooling can ingest records without schema drift.
- Access control: can you provide a simple mapping of roles that can run exports and who is notified when exports occur?

Also, for the pseudonymization: we need to understand whether your hashes are reversible (i.e., do you store salts centrally?) — if so, we need details for our DPO. If the approach is irreversible hashing with a per-customer salt, please document that.

Finally, timeline: procurement wants the sample logs and the residency confirmation in writing to move to a security review. If you can get us the 7-day sample within 5 business days, that should be fine. If not, please state earliest feasible date.

Thanks,
Laura

Neha

What we'll deliver:
- sample-logs-30days.csv.stub (we're generating a 7-day extract first; file will include header and 7 days of synthetic-but-structure-faithful rows with hashed identifiers).

RBAC and audit: exports can be executed by operators in the PlatformOps role and by Redwood Security SREs; every export action emits an audit event (export_id, initiator, timestamp, exported_range) that is retained for 12 months. We will include sample audit events in the deliverable.

ETA: deliverables ready by 2026-11-16 COB (7 business days from 2026-11-09). If procurement needs faster, we can attempt a 5-day turnaround (2026-11-13) but will need Marcus to prioritize infra slots.

Rishi / Marcus — forwarding FinMedia's clarifications. They want a 7-day or 30-day redacted log sample (CSV + JSONL), confirmation of EU storage/backups, retention mapping, and a short description of pseudonymization (hashing + salt management). - Produce 7-day redacted sample first (CSV + JSONL). Include columns Neha listed and an extra payload_has_pii boolean column.
- Provide a 1-paragraph residency statement (eu-west-1 primary, backups in eu-west-1 only / no cross-region replication). If there are exceptions, call them out.
- Short doc on hashing approach (algorithm + where salt is stored / whether reversible).
- Short RBAC note: who can run exports and audit trail for export actions.

Neha

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-09T10:47:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Post-call: PII handling & log sample request (data residency check)

Neha / Marcus — TL;DR: we can deliver the 7-day redacted sample + docs within 7 business days. Plan below.

What we'll deliver:
- sample-logs-30days.csv.stub (we're generating a 7-day extract first; file will include header and 7 days of synthetic-but-structure-faithful rows with hashed identifiers).
- JSONL equivalent for ingestion.
- log-export-process.md (short runbook describing steps, who can run it, required approvals).
- short residency statement: production logs are stored in eu-west-1 and backups are contained in the same region; we do not replicate logs to other regions for customer-selected private-tenanted data.
- hashing/pseudonymization note: we use SHA-256 with a per-customer salt stored in a KMS-backed secrets store. The salt is not reversible by application operators; mapping to cleartext is not retained.

RBAC and audit: exports can be executed by operators in the PlatformOps role and by Redwood Security SREs; every export action emits an audit event (export_id, initiator, timestamp, exported_range) that is retained for 12 months. We will include sample audit events in the deliverable.

ETA: deliverables ready by 2026-11-16 COB (7 business days from 2026-11-09). If procurement needs faster, we can attempt a 5-day turnaround (2026-11-13) but will need Marcus to prioritize infra slots.
