title: 3-year annualized prepay — savings safeguard & ramp proposal
preview:
From: Sara Wallace <sara.wallace@nivushealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 7 Nov 2026 14:20:00 -0800
Subject: 3yr annualized prepay + ramp — initial numbers

Hi Chloe,

Thanks for the product walkthrough yesterday — the team is excited about Redwood’s caching and routing features. We’re evaluating a 3-year annualized prepay to get better unit economics for our inference-heavy pipelines. A couple quick details to help you sketch a first-pass: 

- Current baseline: ~450M tokens/month (Q3 average) across a mix of chat and batch prompts.
- Expected growth: 35% year-over-year driven by two new feature launches (est. mid-2027 and mid-2028).
- Desire: a blended effective discount that protects us if growth is slower (true-up/credit path) but rewards us for committing.

Can you put together a short offer that shows a front-loaded discount vs. evenly annualized, and include a suggested ramp/true-up approach? We’ll need to show procurement and finance two options for internal approval. 

I attached our usage extract (Nivus_usage_2026_Q1-Q3.csv).

Best,
Sara
Sara Wallace
Head of Cloud Procurement | Nivus Health




From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Wallace <sara.wallace@nivushealth.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: 3yr annualized prepay + ramp — initial numbers

Hi Sara — thanks for the data and the context. I pulled together a short, pragmatic offer for your procurement and finance teams. High-level options attached: Redwood-Nivus-3yr-Offer-v1.pdf. Summary below.

Option A — Frontloaded savings (recommended for predictable savings):
- Term: 36 months, annualized billing (customer prepays each year)
- Year 1: 20% unit discount vs list (applied to billed tokens after prefunded allocation)
- Year 2: 15% unit discount
- Year 3: 12% unit discount
- True-up: Quarterly true-up with rollover credits; if actual usage < prepaid allocation, unused dollars convert to a credit applied to the following year; if actual usage > prepaid, billed at agreed discounted unit price.
- Protect: growth buffer credit (5% of year prepaid value) to absorb initial under-utilization in year 1.

Option B — Even annualized (lower up-front impact):
- Year 1: 14% discount, Year 2: 14%, Year 3: 14%
- Simpler accounting, smaller up-front cost.

Notes and asks:
- I modeled the Year 1 frontload to match an objective where you receive most of the financial value early, but we keep a gentle step-down to preserve unit economics for Redwood as utilization grows.
- Need confirmation on how Nivus prefers prepaid cadence (annual upfront vs. multi-year up-front). Your note suggested annualized prepay — I used that in the draft.
- Please confirm whether finance needs a payment schedule (we can do annual or semi-annual).

I’m looping in Karthik from pricing for approvals/guardrails on the frontloaded delta vs. our standard. If this looks directionally good I’ll ask for a one-time approval for the Year 1 uplift.

Attachments: Redwood-Nivus-3yr-Offer-v1.pdf

Best,
Chloe Martin
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
T: 415-555-0124



From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 10:12:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: 3yr annualized prepay + ramp — approval request

Ben / Karthik — forwarding Sara’s ask and the attached draft offer. Quick context: Nivus is a mid-market healthcare platform with heavy inference for triage + summarization. Baseline ~450M tokens/mo and projected ~35% YoY. I recommended Option A (frontloaded) because it helps them hit realized savings in year 1 and is preferred by their CFO.

Requesting a one-time flex to move Year 1 to 20% (our standard for 36m annualized is 14–16%). They’ve committed to annual upfront invoices and quarterly true-up; finance prefers that cadence.

Key trade-offs:
- If we give 20% Year 1, we want year-over-year stepdown to 15% then 12% and a modest SLA credit cap (we'll offer a 5% credit cap for sustained outages).
- We should include a 6-month performance guardrail that allows us to convert to a lower-tier model variant if cost profile materially deviates.

Attachments: redwood-internal-pricing-workup.xlsx

—
Chloe

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 12:08:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: 3yr annualized prepay + ramp — approval request

Chloe — thanks for the context and the workup. I’ve reviewed the internal spreadsheet. Approve exception for Year 1 20% with the following conditions: 

1) Add a 9-month performance review clause (instead of 6 months) to assess model variant routing and economics; we need a slightly longer runway in healthcare customers.
2) SLA credit cap = 5% of annual prepaid value (as you suggested).
3) Include a mid-term true-up floor so that if usage is <60% of projected baseline for the first two quarters of Year 1, we reserve the right to convert Year 1 unused dollars to a one-time credit (instead of rolling forward) — this is to limit long-term exposure on very low utilization.

If Karthik concurs on the numeric sensitivity, proceed to finalize the offer and send to Sara with DocuSign link. Mark the deal as a price-exception in the system (RDW-DEAL-2026-4891).

—Ben

Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference

- Year 2: 15% discount
- Year 3: 12% discount

title: Compliance add-ons — volume tier counterproposal and EU residency bundle
preview:
From: Eric Zhou <eric.zhou@clearhealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Cc: Nina Patel <nina.patel@clearhealth.com>
Date: Fri, 06 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Compliance add-ons: pricing options for ClearHealth pilot

Avery —

Thanks for the demo last week. Two quick follow-ups from our security team:
- We need a line-item breakdown for audit log retention (30/90/365 days) and whether logs can be exported to our S3.
- KMS vs HSM: we expect to require customer-managed keys (KMS) for the pilot, but HSM may be required later. Please include one-time setup + monthly charges.
- EU residency: our EU business unit will hit the pilot and needs EU data residency. What does that cost as an add-on?

Attached is our security questionnaire (redacted). Procurement also asked for a pricing cadence: monthly vs annual committed discounts. We have a 6-month pilot planned starting Jan. What's realistic to lock in?

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call.

Eric
Eric Zhou
Head of Security, ClearHealth Systems


From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
To: Eric Zhou <eric.zhou@clearhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 07 Nov 2026 10:00:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Compliance add-ons: pricing options for ClearHealth pilot

Eric —

Appreciate the detail. Attached is a first-pass spreadsheet (redwood-compliance-pricing-v1.xlsx) with line items and recommended packaging. Highlights:

- Audit logs (per GB/month): 30d = $0.03/GB, 90d = $0.05/GB, 365d = $0.11/GB. Includes optional automated export to customer S3 for an extra $0.01/GB.
- KMS (customer-managed keys): $1,200/mo per tenancy for managed KMS integration; one-time onboarding $4,500.
- HSM (dedicated): one-time provisioning $42k, + $3,500/mo. HSM requires minimum 12-month commitment.
- EU residency: +15% on top of base inference rate for EU-hosted model endpoints; or a residency bundle (see below) for customers with both EU and US traffic.

Packaging suggestion (pilot-friendly):
- Residency bundle (6-month pilot): EU region + audit-log 90d + KMS integration = $6,500/mo (discounted from line-item sum).
- Volume tiers (applies to compliance add-ons across accounts): 0-5TB, 5-25TB (10% off), 25TB+ (20% off).

Concession rule we can offer for a 6-month pilot with a signed PO by 12/15: waive half of the one-time KMS onboarding fee (save $2,250) and provide a one-time $10k implementation credit for residency setup (applied to first invoice).

Questions:
1) For ClearHealth, which parts must be hard requirements for launch vs nice-to-have later? (KMS required? EU residency required day 1?)
2) Do you have an expected audit log volume per month (GB)? This will drive whether tier discounts kick in.

Looping Ben for SRE/implementation timing and Karthik for any compliance-specific notes. If this aligns, I can circulate v2 with a proposed SOW and a DocuSign link.

Best,
Avery Johnson
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-compliance-pricing-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Eric Zhou <eric.zhou@clearhealth.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
Cc: Nina Patel <nina.patel@clearhealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 08 Nov 2026 08:45:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Compliance add-ons: pricing options for ClearHealth pilot

Avery —

Thanks for the fast turnaround. Quick clarifications from our side:
- KMS: required for pilot. We will start with customer-managed KMS but want an option to upgrade to HSM later if traffic justifies it.
- EU residency: required for our EU product line at launch. US can be default.
- Audit logs: anticipating ~3TB/month initially (growing), likely to hit 5TB in month 4 if the pilot expands.
- Procurement note: our finance team prefers monthly invoicing but will consider quarterly if there is a modest commit.

On concessions: the KMS onboarding waiver is helpful. To move forward we need a clearer split of credits vs discounts (we want to see committed discount manifest as reduced monthly line item rather than an isolated credit). Also need to know whether the EU residency bundle can be priced as a flat per-month service (easier for internal budgeting) rather than a % uplift on per-token rates.

Can Marissa or someone from your finance team draft an alternate with: (a) flat EU residency fee option, (b) committed monthly pricing for 6 months for the bundle, and (c) an explicit path/price to upgrade KMS -> HSM (month-to-month prorated)? We can then push to procurement for a draft PO.

If helpful, procurement indicated they can sign a 6-month PO but want clear, flat monthly numbers to include in the PO.

Thanks — Eric

On Sat, Nov 7, 2026 at 10:00 AM Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Eric —
>
> Appreciate the detail. Attached is a first-pass spreadsheet (redwood-compliance-pricing-v1.xlsx) with line items and recommended packaging. Highlights:
> ...



From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.ai>
To: Eric Zhou <eric.zhou@clearhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 09 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Compliance add-ons: pricing options for ClearHealth pilot

Eric / Nina —

Appreciate the clarity — attached is redwood-compliance-pricing-v2.xlsx and a short SOW (residency-sow-draft.pdf) that convert the prior per-token uplift into a flat EU residency monthly fee option for the pilot. Summary of changes:

1) Flat EU residency option (pilot): $5,000/mo covers EU-hosted endpoints + 90d audit log retention for EU traffic + KMS integration for one tenancy. This can be invoiced monthly.

2) KMS pricing path: commit to 6 months and we will: (a) reduce monthly KMS integration to $900/mo (from $1,200) and (b) waive $2,250 of the one-time onboarding fee as a credit applied to month 1.

3) HSM upgrade: one-time provisioning $42k (same as prior), prorated on activation, with a 12-month minimum thereafter. We will provide a 30% discount on provisioning for a signed 12-month commitment within the pilot window.

4) Audit-log volume tiers retained but made additive to the flat residency fee for global logs. If ClearHealth keeps EU traffic in the residency bucket, those logs are included up to 2TB/month; overage billed at the 90d rate ($0.05/GB).

Counterproposal concession: For a 6-month PO signed by 12/15 we will:
- Lock the flat EU residency at $5,000/mo for the pilot period (no % uplifts).
- Apply the $10k implementation credit to invoicing and move the $2,250 onboarding waiver into the invoice as a negative line item (so procurement has flat monthly numbers).

Next steps I propose:
- Confirm acceptance of the flat EU residency fee and the 6-month commitment window.
- If yes, I will: (a) circulate v2 SOW + DocuSign, (b) copy finance to agree on PO wording, (c) schedule an implementation kickoff with Ben and Karthik.

If you'd like a single-row summary for procurement, here's a quick suggested PO line: 
"ClearHealth — 6-month Residency Pilot: $5,000/mo residency + $900/mo KMS integration + audit-log overage as applicable (included 2TB/mo)"

Happy to hop on a 20-min call to finalize wording.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0198

Attachments: redwood-compliance-pricing-v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), residency-sow-draft.pdf (SOW)

title: Transient sync policy, temporary egress window and region-pin commitment
preview:
From: Elena Marquez <dpo@acmecorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Legal <legal@acmecorp.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 15:12:00 +0000
Subject: Data residency question: transient sync windows & pinning commitment

Hi Ava,

Thanks for taking this — quick context: Acme is finalising a purchase order for Redwood Dedicated and our DPO/legal teams need clarity on a couple of operational behaviors to sign the DPA. Two items in particular:

1) Transient cross-region sync / failover windows: our architecture requires that customer-controlled data remains pinned to region where possible (EU for EU customers, APAC for APAC). We understand there are transient syncs for disaster recovery and cache rebuilds. Can you confirm the maximum duration Redwood needs for any automatic cross-region replication or prefix/KV cache rehydration for production traffic? Please specify any differences between (a) user-facing routing/failover and (b) background analytics/telemetry.

2) Documentation & audit evidence: we need a short sample of the audit logs that show region-of-origin, timestamp, and KMS key id used for transient transfer, plus your proposed DPA clause language that lists the permitted exception and maximum timeframe.

If you can provide a draft clause and an example audit line we can share with our legal team today, that would be great. We prefer the transient window to be limited to 24 hours for customer-sensitive traffic.

Elena
Elena Marquez
Data Protection Officer, Acme Corp
---
Attachments: none in this message


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Elena Marquez <dpo@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 7 Nov 2026 09:08:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Data residency question: transient sync windows & pinning commitment

Elena — thanks for the clear ask and sorry for the slightly long reply. Quick TL;DR up front: Redwood can commit to region pinning by default with a controlled, auditable transient sync window for failover and cache rehydration. We propose the following:

- Failover (user-facing routing and KV cache required to restore active sessions): up to 4 hours per incident.
- Analytics/telemetry and background batch rehydration: up to 24 hours per scheduled or incidental transfer.
- All transient cross-region transfers will be logged, encrypted with customer-specific KMS keys (if configured), and retained in audit logs for at least 90 days.

I've attached a draft DPA clause that captures the permitted exception language and a current subprocessor roster. Priyom and Rishi are looped in to confirm operational constraints and the audit log sample.

Please see the attached: redwood-dpa-transient-egress-draft.pdf, subprocessor-roster-2026-10.csv, audit-log-sample-utc.txt

If you prefer 24 hours for all use-cases as you suggested, we can surface that as an option, but we want to be explicit about the different risk envelopes (user routing vs analytics).

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132
Redwood Inference
---
Attachments: redwood-dpa-transient-egress-draft.pdf; subprocessor-roster-2026-10.csv; audit-log-sample-utc.txt


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Elena Marquez <dpo@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Nov 2026 13:27:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Data residency question: transient sync windows & pinning commitment

Thanks Ava — a couple of operational clarifications from infra:

- The 4 hour window for user-facing failover assumes the customer has enabled regional pinning and that there is compatible capacity in the failover region. For cold restores (e.g., when entire node pools are rebuilt), some background KV rehydration may stretch closer to 12 hours depending on model sequence lengths and cache hit patterns. We can make 4 hours the SLO for active-session recovery and declare a hard upper bound of 24 hours for background restores.

- KMS handling: If the customer provides a customer-managed key (CMK) in the target region, all transient copies will be encrypted with that CMK. If not, Redwood encrypts with our managed keys and we note the key id in the audit entry. We will add the KMS key id field to the audit example.

- Subprocessors: the attached roster includes cloud provider regions used for transient syncs. We will flag any new subprocessor changes during procurement.

Suggested clause tweak (short): 
\"Transient cross-border processing may occur only to support (i) user-facing continuity (SLO: 4 hours) and (ii) background rehydration/analytics (hard upper bound: 24 hours). All transfers will be logged with region-of-origin, destination region, timestamp, and KMS key identifier. Transfers will be encrypted in transit and at rest. Redwood will provide audit evidence on request and will seek customer approval for any extension beyond 24 hours except in a court order or emergency incident where immediate legal requirements apply.\"

Let me know if Acme wants the SLO and the hard upper bound changed to 24 hours for both cases — we can do that but want it explicit in the DPA.

— Priyom
Priyom Das
Product Manager, Redwood Inference
---
Attachments referenced: subprocessor-roster-2026-10.csv (already attached by Ava); audit-log-sample-utc.txt (includes KMS key id sample)


From: Elena Marquez <dpo@acmecorp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 10:30:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Data residency question: transient sync windows & pinning commitment

Ava/Priyom — thanks, that helps a lot.

We reviewed the draft clause and the audit sample. Two quick follow-ups:

1) Can you replace the phrase \"hard upper bound\" with \"maximum permitted window\" in the clause text? Legal wants language that is actionable and not open to reinterpretation.

2) We need a one-page audit log sample showing a realistic transient transfer event (fields: event_id, customer_id, region_of_origin, destination_region, start_ts, end_ts, bytes_transferred, kms_key_id, initiating_agent). The attached audit-log-sample-utc.txt covers most but please update to include bytes_transferred in the example and re-send.

If you update the draft clause to use \"maximum permitted window\" and re-attach the updated audit sample, Acme Legal will proceed with DocuSign. Please include the Drive link for the DPA draft and we will sign once DocuSign is received.

Elena
Elena Marquez
Data Protection Officer, Acme Corp

title: Proposal: Astrell outage mitigation & goodwill package
preview:
From: engineering-lead@astrell.com
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@astrell.com
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 23:12:00 -0800
Subject: Production latency spike - impact to clinical workflows

Hi Ben —

We're seeing sustained 95th percentile latency of 2.8s (vs. our SLA target of 300ms) across several inference routes since ~08:10 UTC this morning. This has caused delayed responses in our intake queue and a couple of patient-facing retries in the mobile app. We've attached a short timeline and the raw metrics.

This is material for us; our engineers are standing by and we'd like a remediation/compensation plan while you investigate root cause. Please escalate — our procurement team is on cc for commercial follow-up.

Thanks,
Arjun Patel
Engineering Lead, Astrell Health

Attachments: latency-metrics-20261105.csv, prod-incident-timeline.pdf

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: engineering-lead@astrell.com, procurement@astrell.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 06 Nov 2026 07:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Production latency spike - impact to clinical workflows

Hi Arjun —

Really sorry to hear this. I pulled the incident summary from our side and engaged the incident response team; they're still validating whether this was a regional networking flake or a serving regression from a nightly rollout.

Immediate next steps from my side:
- I'm asking the team to prioritize a rollback if we confirm a rollout impact.
- I'll get an initial RCA estimate by EOD today.
- For commercial housekeeping: I'm proposing a temporary makeup package while we finalize the RCA so you can triage with procurement.

I want to confirm what would be most helpful for you operationally: a dollar credit applied to this invoice, a one-month free period of Dedicated capacity to avoid throttling while we stabilize, or a hybrid (partial credit + risk-free Dedicated trial). If you tell me the preferred form of remediation I can fast-track an approval request.

Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 06 Nov 2026 10:14:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Production latency spike - impact to clinical workflows — need approval for mitigation package

Folks — forwarding Arjun's thread and the attached metrics. Asking for exec-level sign-off on a mitigation package so I can reply to procurement quickly.

Quick context: Astrell is a mid-market payer with increasing usage; they rely on a real-time inference route (patient-intake). The impact window looks to be ~45 minutes but with customer-visible retries.

Options I'm proposing (order of preference from sales):
A) Two-month Dedicated trial (zero charge) + $12k invoice credit applied to Nov billing if RCA shows platform regression.
B) One-month Dedicated trial + $20k unconditional credit (no RCA dependence).
C) $30k credit only (conditional on confirmed service regression).

Rationale: Dedicated trial reduces chance of recurrence while we finish any config/rollout fixes; the dollar figures are intended to cover the customer's operational risk and retention economics. Asking Marissa/Karthik to confirm which option we can approve quickly (legal review will follow for T&Cs).

Ben

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 07 Nov 2026 09:51:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production latency spike - impact to clinical workflows — need approval for mitigation package

Thanks for the summary. A few notes:
- We should avoid an unconditional high-value credit if the incident is clearly attributable to customer configuration. Confirm whether Astrell changed request patterns or model context size prior to the window.
- The Dedicated trial (Option A) is attractive commercially — it demonstrates good faith and helps them stabilize without immediate cash impact. However, two months zero-charge is meaningful from a revenue standpoint; I want Karthik's take on whether we can pair that with a smaller invoice credit.

If we approve A, propose adjusting the $12k credit to $8k and require automatic acceptance of a standard portability clause for trial capacity (30-day notice). Legal will need a redline but we can do an expedited approval if Karthik confirms.

Marissa
Head of Sales Operations | Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 07 Nov 2026 11:26:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production latency spike - impact to clinical workflows — need approval for mitigation package

Agree with Marissa. My recommendation: Approve Option A with the adjustment (two-month Dedicated trial + $8k invoice credit), contingent on legal sign-off of the trial portability clause. We'll add a condition that credits are only applied if our RCA determines platform-side regression or if we cannot detect customer-side misconfiguration within 5 business days.

I can greenlight that immediately from a retention perspective; please copy me on the legal redline and the doc for customer signature. Ben — if Astrell accepts A, let's also open a short performance review engagement (2-session runbook) so CSM can reduce recurrence.

Karthik
Director, Customer Success | Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: engineering-lead@astrell.com, procurement@astrell.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>

title: Dedicated capacity floorplan & surge architecture - Lumos Bio
preview:
From: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency numbers looked strong for our baseline load. We’re starting to firm up our infrastructure ask for Q2 2027 and want to include Redwood Dedicated in the procurement packet. Before we loop procurement I need a concise set of reservation options we can present to our leadership and infra team. Goal: keep cost predictable while guaranteeing low tail latency during daytime clinical workloads.

Quick context:
- Expected steady-state inference: ~160 concurrent requests during daytime peaks (US EST 8a-6p).
- Typical prompt length: 512 tokens. Models: proprietary 13B family (we may finetune), plan to run 1–2 variants.
- Traffic pattern: weekday peaks with occasional scheduled burst testing windows (max 4x baseline for short windows).
- Internal compliance: must be VPC-connected and have audit log retention for 1 year.

What we need from you: three capacity options laid out as a simple "floorplan" table — minimum committed GPUs, burst/scale options, recommended ramp schedule and activation timeline, and any blocker items for on-prem vs. VPC deployment. Attachments or one-sheet preferred for exec review.

Target timeline: decision by end of November.

Thanks — Joel
Joel Pattinson
Head of Platform
Lumos Bio

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Joel — appreciate the concise context. Attached are three pragmatic reservation floorplans and a short SOW stub to help procurement: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx and Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf. High level summary below so you can paste into the packet.

Option A — Pilot / Low-commit (90-day)- Minimum commit: 8 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: up to 32 GPUs for scheduled test windows (auto-burst policy)- Ramp: immediate activation of 8 GPUs, burst cap available within 48–72 hours provisioning window- Use case: validate finetune + production smoke testing- Pricing: monthly reserved baseline + per-burst usage. Estimated monthly baseline cost range: $XXk–$YYk

Option B — Business (recommended for stable prod)- Minimum commit: 32 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 64 GPUs available via short-term capacity lanes and scheduled bursts- Ramp: stage activation over 8 weeks (32 -> 48 -> 64 bursts enabled), full committed baseline online in 2 weeks- Use case: primary production serving with predictable latency and 2 model variants- Pricing: lower per-token unit cost vs. Option A; we include a 10% startup credit on first two months for a 12-month commit

Option C — Enterprise / Large-commit- Minimum commit: 96 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 128 GPUs with multi-region fallback- Ramp: staged over 12–20 weeks with canary lanes and A/B traffic steering- Use case: multi-product rollout, strict SLOs and geo-bursting

Notes / restrictions:
- VPC deployment: includes private control plane, KMS integration; on-prem air-gap requires additional security assessment (see SOW).
- Audit logs: 12-month retention available as an add-on; standard retention is 90 days.
- Network: we recommend 10GbE minimum egress for the baseline 32-GPU option; see Ben’s note in the next message.

If you want, we can convert Option B into a one-page commitment slide for procurement and include estimated burn numbers. Which option should I flesh out first?

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx; Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf

---


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 14:43:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts — technical sizing notes

Joel / Aisha — adding a short technical annex to support the Options above. I attached network_requirements.docx with a brief checklist. Key sizing notes: 

1) GPU selection and effective throughput- For your 13B family we estimate ~13–16 tokens/sec per A100-80GB-equivalent GPU under float16 serving with KV cache. That maps to ~10–12 concurrent steady requests per GPU at 512 tokens average (depending on batching).

2) Minimum recommended baselines (conservative rule-of-thumb):- 8 GPUs: safe for pilots and small smokes (~80–100 qps sustained with aggressive batching)- 32 GPUs: production baseline for 160 concurrent daytime users at low latency assuming request coalescing and prefix caching- 96 GPUs: multi-product or multi-variant fleets with headroom for retraining jobs and throughput spikes

3) Burst and ramp behavior- We recommend configuring both automated burst lanes and a short-notice provisioning policy. Autofill burst usually requires a 24–72 hour warm-up window. For your scheduled test windows we can provision guaranteed burst lanes that come online in under 4 hours if pre-committed.

4) Network and storage- Baseline egress: 10Gbps for 32-GPU baseline. For 96 GPUs plan for 25–40Gbps depending on expected KV cache miss rates.

5) Safety and rollout- Canary lanes with sample-based quality monitoring; automatic fallbacks to smaller model variants if latency or error budgets breach.

If helpful I can run a quick capacity simulation with your request traces (anonymized) to produce expected percentiles for P50/P95/P99 latency against each option.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

Attachments: network_requirements.docx

---

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>

1) Please send a one-page commitment slide + the SOW with pricing filled in so procurement can attach to the PO.
2) Please confirm DocuSign timeline — we need the signed agreement by Dec 3 to meet our kickoff date in mid-Jan.

We prefer the 12-month commit with the 10% startup credit you flagged. Also confirm the VPC deployment includes KMS integration and 12-month audit log add-on (we’ll cover the add-on cost).

title: Capacity decision & rollout checkpoint for CardioSense Dedicated Pool
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.inference>
To: Thomas Li <thomas.li@cardiosense.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference>
Date: 2026-11-07T15:02:00-08:00
Subject: Follow-up: dedicated capacity recap & immediate asks

Thomas — thanks for taking the call earlier. Quick recap from our side so we’re aligned and can move to proposal: 

What you stated as the main pain points:
- Frequent latency spikes during peak inference windows (even with smaller batch sizes) impacting SLA to clinicians.
- Need strong tenant isolation for PHI workflows (multi-tenant model serving today but want dedicated pool).
- On-prem-ish networking requirement: VPC peering + BYOK for KMS; audit logs & retention for 12 months.

Goals you asked us to hit:
- P95 latency target: <= 120ms for 512-token requests in steady-state.
- Baseline throughput: ~300 requests/min steady with burst to 1k reqs/min during overnight backfills.
- Go-live target window: early Jan (you mentioned Jan 8–15).

Preliminary sizing notes (attached sizing worksheet):
- Recommended reserved baseline: 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (quantized 4-bit run for most models).
- Burst pool: 2 GPUs for short-term spikes (auto-burst to shared capacity if required).
- Canary plan: Gradual traffic ramp (10% → 50% → 100%) with automatic fallback to hosted model variant.

SLA & security: we can offer 99.9% uptime SLA for the dedicated pool + SOC2 evidence and audit logs. BYOK + VPC private control plane supported; we’ll include Ops acceptance checklist in the next doc.

Next steps we committed to on call:
1) I’ll share an initial commercial + technical sizing (attached).
2) Schedule a technical deep-dive (security + infra) for the week of Nov 14.
3) Draft mutual action plan for signatures and timeline (first cut attached).

Can you confirm: the go-live target window above still works and the procurement contact is procurement@cardiosense.ai? If anything above is off, call it out and I’ll update the sizing doc.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0193

-- attachments: call_recording_20261107.mp3; initial_sizing_estimate_cardiosense.xlsx; mudp_draft_cardiosense.pdf

- Timeline: procurement prefers a Jan 10 go-live target; that gives us time for an internal security review.

title: DevRel + Marketplace pipeline: onboarding checklist and co-sell readiness
preview:
From: Emma Li <emma.li@stratus-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@stratus-analytics.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Interested in Redwood listing + co-sell — what do you need from us?

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the latency and routing features look very promising for our inference-heavy pipeline. Our partnerships lead asked me to start the marketplace listing process so we can move toward joint selling with your channel partners.

High level asks from our side:
- What evidence artifacts do you require for a GCP/Azure listing (security, billing plans, integrations)?
- Do you have a standard mutual action plan for co-sell enablement and case studies?
- Who from Redwood will own the initial technical validation and go-to-market announcement?

I've looped procurement for contracting and Jordan from CloudBridge (our integrator) will likely need to be on technical calls. Can you send a checklist and an estimated timeline?

Best,
Emma
Emma Li
Sr. Partnerships Manager, Stratus Analytics
[Attachment: Company_Brief_Stratus.pdf (pdf, 210KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Li <emma.li@stratus-analytics.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 11:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood listing + co-sell — what do you need from us?

Thanks Emma — great to meet you.

Short answer: we can do both listing and co-sell; the typical path is evidence -> technical validation -> commercial alignment -> enablement. I've attached our pared-down evidence checklist and a draft mutual action plan to get started.

To answer your bullets:
- Evidence: SOC2 report (latest), IP & data flow diagram, sample SLA/capacity map, billing/SKU mapping for metered usage. We accept REDACTED or vendor-hosted links for sensitive items.
- Mutual action plan: see attached PDF — it includes a 6–8 week target for public listing if evidence is complete and legal review is standard.
- Owners: Olga will run the evidence intake and technical validation; Karthik will coordinate co-sell enablement and marketplace policies. I will be the commercial lead for the launch comms.

Next steps proposed:
1) Emma/Jordan: fill the first tab in the attached checklist with links to docs (or upload to the shared Drive).
2) Olga: triage evidence and schedule a tech validation call with CloudBridge.
3) Legal/procurement: draft MPA and route for signature — Ben will follow up on pricing/SKU questions.

I'll coordinate a 30-min kickoff for Friday; does 10am PT work for your team?

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Marketplace_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx (xlsx, 42KB)]

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Li <emma.li@stratus-analytics.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@cloudbridge.io>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 08:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: DevRel + Marketplace pipeline: onboarding checklist and co-sell readiness

Hi Emma — thanks for looping Jordan.

I've reviewed the checklist Ben sent. A couple clarifications before the kickoff call:
- For the SOC2 artifact we need the auditor statement and the date; a link to your attestation PDF is fine.
- Please include a short diagram showing where customer data flows into CloudBridge vs. Redwood (redact customer names if needed).
- For the SKU mapping: Cloud marketplaces require a usage unit (e.g., vCPU-hours or token-unit) — I've attached a CSV template to speed that mapping.

Jordan — can CloudBridge provide the integration diagram and the sample billing telemetry (even anonymized)? That will speed validation.

If you can't get the SOC2 in the first week, we can do a limited private listing to start partner pilots while the audit is being finalized — worth discussing on the call.

-Olga
Olga Petrov
Partner Operations, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: SKU_Mapping_Draft.csv (csv, 4KB)]

- Can Redwood provide a short co-sell one-pager (1 page) that we can include in our partner playbook?
- Who will be the speaker on the joint announcement webinar (marketing wants a technical + product presence)?

I'll upload links to the Drive folder here once Jordan posts the diagram.

Thanks,
Emma

title: Post-demo: CareVista eval ops & regression guardrails
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Patel <samira.patel@carevista.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-05T15:12:00-08:00
Subject: Post-demo recap + next steps (CareVista / Redwood)

Hi Samira — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap + proposed next steps so we can get a targeted eval running: 

1) Scope & success metrics
- Primary use case: triage summarization + recommended care path classification for inbound patient notes (batch nightly).
- Success metrics you mentioned: label-level F1 for primary diagnosis suggestions >= 0.78, latency budget for batch reranking per record < 200ms (p95) in production.

2) Recommended immediate evals (what we can run on Redwood):
- Sanity run: 500 de-identified sample notes (random mix, clinical/administrative).
- Main benchmark: 5k examples split by clinic type; metrics: top-1 accuracy, macro F1, and hallucination rate by rule-based checks.

3) Model recommendations (initial):
- For quality-first: falcon-40b-instruct (quantized) as reference
- For cost/latency trade-off: mosaic-13b-int8 with prefix KV caching
- Fallback / smaller: redwood-mini-3b for edge tests

4) Data format we need (attached template): carevista_eval_dataset_template.csv — columns: id, source_type, prompt, ground_truth, metadata_tags. Please de-id PHI before sending.

5) Scoring criteria / guardrails:
- Exact-match for structured fields; relaxed-match (normalize dates/units) for free-text comparisons.
- Regression alert thresholds: >3% drop on macro F1 or >5% increase in hallucination triggers (defined in playbook) vs baseline should trigger investigation.

6) Logistics / timeline
- If you can drop the 500-sample sanity set by EOD Tue (11/10), we can spin the sanity run and share results within 48 hours.
- Full 5k run target: week of 11/17 pending data transfer and legal approval.

Attachments: carevista_eval_dataset_template.csv (CSV) and Redwood eval playbook link.

Ask: Can you confirm who will own the dataset handoff (engineering or data governance) and whether we should route transfer to your S3 or use our Secure Upload? Also, would you like us to include a pre-built scoring notebook?

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference




From: Samira Patel <samira.patel@carevista.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement <procurement@carevista.com>
Date: 2026-11-06T09:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + next steps (CareVista / Redwood)

Thanks Ben — good summary. A couple quick replies: 

- I'll own the dataset handoff but will need a short checklist for de-identification. Can you confirm the expected columns and an example? (We saw the template attached.)
- Procurement looped in for SOW/contract language; procurement@carevista.com is on copy.
- For the sanity set: engineering can drop the 500 samples to an S3 bucket we control (s3://carevista-eval-upload). We'll share IAM details after the NDA is finalized.

One immediate concern from clinical QA: hallucination tolerance is near-zero for diagnosis suggestions. Can you outline what mechanisms Redwood uses to detect and flag hallucinations during the eval? Also, will inference logs include token-level traces we can sample?

Samira Patel
Head of Data Science
CareVista Health



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Patel <samira.patel@carevista.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-06T11:44:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + next steps (CareVista / Redwood)

Samira — great questions. Short answers below; longer detail attached in the playbook PDF.

- De-id checklist: remove direct identifiers (names, MRNs), replace with tokens; keep clinical context (meds, vitals). The attached CSV template has a small example row.
- Hallucination detection: we run multi-pronged checks during eval runs: 1) rule-based entity mismatch (e.g., suggested diagnosis not in input history when it should be); 2) n-gram overlap and semantic-similarity heuristics against ground truth; 3) an ensemble classifier trained on labeled hallucination examples to surface likely hallucinations. We return a per-example hallucination flag and a confidence score.
- Logs / traces: yes — Redwood can provide request/response pairs, token-level logits on a sampled subset (policy-controlled), and KV-cache metadata for debugging. These are provided via a secure artifacts bundle (attachments stub) and pulled only with a signed data access agreement.

Notes on scoring thresholds we recommend for the initial run (also in playbook):
- Macro F1 baseline target: 0.78 (flag if drop > 0.03)
- Hallucination flag rate: target < 1.5% (flag if > 5%)
- Latency: sanity run median < 90ms, p95 < 220ms on dedicated instance profile.

Attachments (stubs): redwood_eval_playbook_v1.2.pdf (PDF, includes scoring notebook), quick-scoring-notebook.ipynb (Jupyter).

Next steps I suggest: 
- You upload the 500-sample CSV to s3://carevista-eval-upload by 11/10.
- We'll perform the sanity run and return a written run report + scoring artifacts within 48 hours (will include hallucination breakdown and per-model comparisons).

If helpful I can also schedule a 30-min technical onboarding with Marissa and Karthik to walk the team through the playbook and the scoring notebook.

Best,
Ben

On Fri, Nov 6, 2026 at 09:03 AM Samira Patel <samira.patel@carevista.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — good summary. A couple quick replies:
>
> - I'll own the dataset handoff ...
>


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Patel <samira.patel@carevista.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + next steps (CareVista / Redwood)

Hi Samira — looping in a couple operational details so the run is smooth on our side.

1) Dataset file format checks (we'll run automated validation on upload):
- Required headers: id, source_type, prompt, ground_truth, metadata_tags (as Ben mentioned).
- Acceptable file types: UTF-8 CSV or newline-delimited JSON (we prefer CSV for sampling hooks).
- Max record size: 16k chars per prompt field; any longer should be truncated with a note in metadata_tags.

2) Scoring rubric we will apply (human-review tie-breakers in playbook):
- Primary metrics: macro F1 for categorical outputs, top-1 accuracy where applicable, hallucination rate.
- Secondary: BLEU/ROUGE for summary-like outputs, normalized edit distance for structured fields.
- Human spot-check: we sample 1% of outputs for manual review focusing on safety-critical categories.

3) Regression guardrails / alerts:
- We’ll compute per-batch deltas vs your baseline and generate an alert if any flagged metric crosses thresholds (Ben's thresholds above).
- For hallucination spikes we also provide per-example provenance traces and the ensemble classifier score to prioritize triage.

4) Deliverables for the sanity run:
- Run report (PDF) with top-level metrics and per-model summary.
- Scoring artifacts: CSV of predictions, flags, and per-example scores; sampled token-level traces (redacted).

5) Scheduling: we have a 30-min slot available Mon 11/10 10:00–10:30 PT for the technical onboarding. Does that work for engineering?

Action items for CareVista: confirm S3 path + IAM role and upload EOD 11/10 if possible. Also indicate whether the 1% human spot-check can use your internal clinicians or if Redwood should spin an independent panel.

Thanks — Marissa
Marissa Cole
Solutions Engineering Manager
Redwood Inference

> > Samira — great questions. Short answers below; longer detail attached in the playbook PDF.

preview:
From: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: tax@solastix.com
Date: 2026-11-05T09:12:00Z
Subject: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Hi Aisha,

We have three outstanding Redwood invoices that are in our AP queue: INV-2026-0893, -0894 and -0895. Our local treasury needs confirmation of Solastix's tax treatment before we release payment — specifically: a copy of Redwood's EU VAT registration or self-billing agreement showing reverse-charge handling, and a brief note stating whether Redwood will be invoicing net of VAT under reverse charge.

I attached what we have on file (VAT_Registration_Scan.pdf) but our team says the scanned cert lacks the jurisdictional stamp. Can you confirm what additional evidence would satisfy EU/UK treasury so we can process the payment this week? Also let us know if you accept payment net-of-VAT in our region, or if a VAT-inclusive invoice will be reissued.

Thanks,
Claire
Accounts Payable
Solastix Analytics Ltd
attachments: VAT_Registration_Scan.pdf


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Claire Hwang <accounts@solastix.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-05T12:04:00Z
Subject: Re: Outstanding invoices + VAT residency evidence request

Claire —

Thanks for the note and for sending the scan. We're keen to get this cleared for you. Short answer: we can enable reverse-charge (net-of-VAT) for Solastix once we have: 1) a clear jurisdictional VAT registration (with issuing authority stamp or a tax authority portal screenshot showing active status), and 2) a signed self-billing or billing authorization confirming Solastix will account for VAT locally.

What we'll do next: Ben (cc'd) will check our invoicing system for the issuing entity on the three invoices and confirm which legal entity issued them; Marissa will prepare a brief attestation from Redwood Finance confirming our treatment and whether we can reissue net invoices if needed.

If you can provide a tax authority portal screenshot (EU VAT VIES or UK gov gateway) for Solastix showing their VAT number active, that will dramatically speed things up. If you can't, the stamped VAT certificate or a signed letter from your tax/legal team is acceptable.

Proposed timeline:
- Today EOD (Redwood): confirm issuing entity + initial attestation (Ben/Marissa)
- Claire: upload authority screenshot or stamped cert by 2026-11-06 17:00 CET
- Once docs received: Redwood to reissue net-of-VAT invoices or provide self-billing agreement within 48h and request payment release.

If the treasury requires, we can also arrange a short call with our tax counsel — let us know.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Head of Revenue Ops, Redwood Inference
phone: +1-415-555-0162

title: Re: Intermittent duplicated responses from Hosted API
MSA & Order Form: SLA redlines — maintenance schedule, credit escalation, and response SLA
preview:
From: Alex Gomez <procurement@stratoscloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Redwood MSA/Order Form — SLA redlines attached

Ben —

Thanks for the call this morning. Attaching our redlines to the SLA section of the MSA and the Order Form for your review (see Stratos_SLA_redline_v3.pdf). High level asks from procurement/legal:

- Raise uptime to 99.95% (monthly measurement) or provide a clear business rationale if you cannot meet that.
- Tiered service credits starting at 5% for a single outage month, scaling to 50% for repeated misses — we want a true escalation ladder.
- Shorter P1 response SLA (30 min initial response) and an objective definition for P1/P2.
- Maintenance windows limited to weekends and require >72 hours advance notice; scheduled maintenance should be excluded only with express agreement and a firm blackout calendar for our busiest dates.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept these or propose a counter? We are trying to get legal sign-off before we onboard next quarter.

Alex
Alex Gomez
Sr. Procurement Manager | StratosCloud
procurement@stratoscloud.com

Attachments: Stratos_SLA_redline_v3.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Gomez <procurement@stratoscloud.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:28:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood MSA/Order Form — SLA redlines attached

Alex —

Thanks for sending the redlines and for the clarity on priorities. We're aligned on the desire for predictable behavior; a few immediate notes and a proposed middle-ground so we can move the doc along quickly:

1) Uptime target: Redwood's standard hosted SKU is 99.9% monthly. We can propose a "premium" tier for 99.95% (with a small uplift) or include an availability credit curve that effectively narrows the delta. Can we discuss expected traffic shape so we size the premium uplift?

2) Credits: We are willing to agree a tiered credit ladder, but we prefer a cap that is expressed as a percentage of monthly recurring fees rather than months of fees. Proposed: 5% -> 15% -> 30% for successive failures within a 3-month rolling window, capped at 50%/month overall.

3) Response SLAs: For P1 we can commit to an initial acknowledgement within 60 minutes and engaged remediation within 4 hours for hosted. I know you asked for 30 minutes — to get to that level we need dedicated support (Reserved or Enterprise support add-on).

4) Maintenance: We can limit standard maintenance to weekends and provide 72h advance notice. Two caveats: (a) emergency maintenance (security patching) must be allowed with as much notice as possible, and (b) we need an agreed definition of blackout dates (we can honor up to 6 blackout dates per 12 months).

Next steps I propose: Lauren to take a legal pass on the redlines, Marissa to confirm operational feasibility (maintenance cadence and blackout enforcement), and if needed Karthik to confirm technical constraints on maintenance windows and rolling upgrades. I'll prepare a single redline that captures the above compromises and share by EOD.

Ben Carter
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: RI_proposed_orderform_redlines.docx


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Alex Gomez <procurement@stratoscloud.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 13:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood MSA/Order Form — SLA redlines attached

Ben / Marissa —

Thanks Ben — I'll take the lead on a redline. A few legal points we should address before we send a counter:

- Measurement window and credit calculation: the contract must specify whether uptime is measured per calendar month or over a 30-day trailing window, and whether short interruptions (under 60s) are excluded. My recommendation: monthly periods with a 60s noise floor.

- Cap mechanics: prefer percentage-of-monthly-invoice cap (Ben's suggestion) but need to set the trigger for "successive failures" precisely. Suggest we define an SLI breach and count occurrences within a 90-day rolling window.

- Maintenance carveouts: we should avoid a broad "emergency maintenance" carveout. Instead: (1) define emergency as CVE-critical with exploit proof-of-concept OR legal/regulatory mandates; (2) require best-effort notice and a post-incident report.

- P1 definition: propose the following language to reduce back-and-forth: "P1: production endpoint unavailable for >90% of traffic for >5 consecutive minutes and causing customer-facing failure." This is objective and measurable.

I'll incorporate these into the RD_proposed_orderform_redlines.docx and circulate a tracked PDF. Ben, please confirm the blackout dates cap (you suggested up to 6/year) and whether we are comfortable guaranteeing the 72h notice for scheduled maintenance.

Lauren Bishop
Sr. Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 09:16:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood MSA/Order Form — SLA redlines attached

Ben / team —

Operational feasibility notes from Eng:

- Maintenance windows: We can do rolling upgrades that keep the service online for >99.9% of customers if we limit per-cluster maintenance to 2–4 hours and do staged rollouts. For customers with strict blackout dates, we can schedule maintenance outside their blackout window but that increases coordination overhead.

- Suggested standard maintenance window: Sun 02:00–06:00 PT. That provides a predictable slot for both parties. We can offer an alternative for customers in APAC regions.

- Response SLAs: Achieving a 30-minute P1 initial response requires a dedicated on-call escalation (Enterprise support). For hosted standard customers, the 60-minute ack + 4-hour remediation is realistic if we include automated health checks and an SRE rotation.

- Measurement: Uptime should be based on per-endpoint health checks aggregated at the customer account level; we can provide a reproducible query and export for transparency. I'll attach a short diagram showing the rollout pattern.

Karthik
Principal Engineer, Runtime Ops | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: maintenance_rollout_diagram.png


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alex Gomez <procurement@stratoscloud.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 16:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Redwood MSA/Order Form — SLA redlines attached

Alex —

Appreciate the quick turnaround. Summary of what Redwood will propose in our counter (attached):

- Availability: Offer a Premium Hosted plan that targets 99.95% monthly availability (with a 12% list price uplift), otherwise standard hosted SKU remains 99.9%.
- Credit ladder: 5% -> 15% -> 30% per month for successive breaches within a 90-day rolling window, capped at 50% of monthly invoice. Credits calculated as a percent of monthly recurring fees.
- P1/P2: We will adopt Lauren's objective P1 definition. Commitment: 60 minute initial acknowledgement / 4 hour engaged remediation on hosted. 30 minute initial ack option for Enterprise (dedicated on-call).
- Maintenance: Standard maintenance limited to Sun 02:00–06:00 PT, 72 hour notice. Emergency maintenance narrowly defined (CVE-critical or legal/regulatory demand) with post-incident report. Up to 6 blackout dates honored per 12-month period.

Next steps: Lauren will send the tracked redline PDF by EOD; Marissa will append the operational cadence to the SOW; Karthik will add the rollout diagram and the SLI query for transparency. If this is acceptable, please confirm and we'll route to DocuSign (link below) and update HubSpot (deal HB-9876).

DocuSign (preview): https://www.docusign.com/redirect?token=0a1b2c3d4e
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/987654321/HB-9876

Ben Carter
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: RI_proposed_orderform_redlines.docx, maintenance_rollout_diagram.png

preview:
From: Matt Quinn <m.quinn@stellarretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Pilot: cross-cloud listing + private offers for early access

Hi Camila,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the latency and cost profile looked promising for our storefront personalization pilot. We want to move quickly and get a private-offer + listing up in both AWS and Azure marketplaces for a 6-month early access pilot (North America only).

Ask: can Redwood provide a bundled concession for the pilot: a blended marketplace listing credit of ~40% first-year equivalent (applied to committed pilot spend) and waiver of the one-time listing fee? We need the pricing concession spelled out in a private offer so our procurement team can approve.

Attached is a high-level usage forecast and draft SOW (pilot_SOW.pdf / usage_forecast.xlsx). If you need any more detail, I can loop procurement (cc'd).

Best,
Matt Quinn
Head of Product
StellarRetail
procurement@stellarretail.com

--
Attachment: usage_forecast.xlsx (spreadsheet, 42KB)
Attachment: pilot_SOW.pdf (pdf, 128KB)

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Pilot: cross-cloud listing + private offers for early access

Ben / Lauren — forwarding StellarRetail's request. Key points: cross-cloud (AWS + Azure) private offers + marketplace listing; ask is ~40% blended credit + listing fee waiver for initial pilot. Attachments from the prospect included.

Why this is time-sensitive: they want procurement approval next week to start a Dec pilot. Volume estimate inside usage_forecast.xlsx looks modest (pilot committed spend ~ $120k over 6 months across both clouds).

My recommendation for deal desk consideration:
- Approve a one-time bundled concession equal to 30% of first-year equivalent on committed pilot spend (capped at $50k total concession),

title: Problem: Supplier portal login blocked by cookie banner (SupplyLane)
preview:
From: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: SupplyLane IT <it-support@supplylane.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Grace,

We're trying to finish the vendor setup for the SupplyLane account but hit a blocker on the supplier portal. When we click the 'Accept & Continue' button in the embedded login iframe the cookie consent banner (site-level) appears and overlays the iframe. The iframe's 'Accept' button becomes unresponsive — looks like third-party cookies are blocked by our browser policy.

I attached a screenshot (supplylane-screenshot-iframe.png). We're on Chrome 119.0.6045 on macOS 13 and tried in Safari with the same result. The inflight PO (PO-9921) is pending so this is blocking our procurement team from completing payment setup.

Can Redwood provide a workaround? A one-click top-level activation link would help while you investigate.

Thanks,
Emily Cho
Vendor Ops | SupplyLane, Inc.
attachments: supplylane-screenshot-iframe.png


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:38:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Emily —

Thanks for the clear repro and the screenshot — sorry for the friction here. I've created an internal ticket (SUP-1228) and looped in Ben (infra) and Jordan (onboard UX).

Quick answers/next steps:
- Ben: can you confirm if the portal iframe is setting any SameSite cookie attributes that would break in an embedded frame? If so, can we issue a short-lived top-level activation URL?
- Emily: as a temporary workaround, if you can open the activation link in a new tab (not the embedded frame) it often bypasses third-party cookie restrictions. I'll send a one-time activation link if we can generate it.

I'll report back within the hour once Ben has a technical read.

Best,
Grace O'Connor
Sr. Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

On Thu, Nov 5, 2026 at 9:38 AM Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Emily —
> Thanks for the clear repro and the screenshot — sorry for the friction here. I've created an internal ticket (SUP-1228)...

We've reproduced this in-house. Root cause: the supplier portal uses a top-level consent banner that sets a cookie with SameSite=Lax and additionally relies on a third-party cookie for the embedded auth flow. When the portal is embedded as an iframe (customer sites often do this in procurement UIs) the browser treats the auth cookie as third-party and blocks it; the consent banner is rendered in the parent context and steals focus, making the iframe controls inert.

Temporary remediation we can do immediately:
- Generate a one-time, top-level activation link that opens outside of the iframe and completes the consent flow. This avoids third-party cookie constraints.
- Also sending cookie headers snippet from our reproduction so SupplyLane can share with their IT team; attached as cookie-headers.txt.

Longer-term fixes to propose:
1) Update portal to set SameSite=None; Secure on auth cookies and ensure explicit SameSite=None consent for embedded flows.
2) Provide a small JS postMessage handshake so parent pages can surface the consent banner without stealing focus from the iframe controls.

I'll generate the one-time activation link now and paste it here in a secure HubSpot note (HUB-45321). Grace — want me to issue the link to Emily directly or have you forward?

—Ben
Ben Carter
SRE, Inference Platform
Redwood Inference
attachments: cookie-headers.txt


From: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: SupplyLane IT <it-support@supplylane.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Grace / Ben —

Thanks — the top-level activation link you provided worked. We opened the link in a new tab and the onboarding finished (PO-9921 now shows as active in our vendor portal). Confirmed with our IT team: they saw the SameSite header in the request and will coordinate to whitelist the Redwood auth cookie or accept the postMessage approach you suggested.

Can you please add SUP-1228 to the roadmap for a permanent fix? We'd like to avoid manual workarounds for future suppliers who embed the portal. HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/45321 (linked above).

Appreciate the quick help.

Best,
Emily Cho
Vendor Ops | SupplyLane, Inc.

title: Leadership recap: AtlasAI discovery — outcomes, risks, and procurement path
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Matthews <jmatthews@atlasai.com>, Priya Sood <priya.sood@atlasai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Leadership recap: AtlasAI discovery — outcomes, risks, and procurement path

Thanks Jordan and Priya — great conversation today. Below is a concise leadership-oriented recap (so your exec sponsor / procurement team can scan quickly), plus next steps from Redwood.

High-level business outcomes discussed
- Improve customer support automation: target 30% reduction in manual triage time within 3 months post-pilot.
- Reduce inference cost per query by ~3x through batching, KV caching, and model selection.
- Enable PII-safe routing: hybrid private deployment for PII workflows and hosted for lower-sensitivity traffic.

Top pain points we heard
- Latency variability during peak (affects agent handoffs and SLA for key customers).
- Cost unpredictability across bursty workloads.
- Security & compliance: need SOC 2, data residency controls, and clear egress/retention policy.
- Vendor lock-in concerns / desire for model portability.

Recommended pilot & procurement path (agreed on call)
1) Pilot (6 weeks): dedicated pool + sample traffic, defined success metrics (attached).
2) Security review: deliver SOC 2 artifacts, pen-test summary, architecture diagram.
3) SOW + pricing: draft SOW from Redwood -> procurement redlines -> PO.
4) Production roll: canary rollout with fallback to hosted model variants.

Executive sponsor(s) and procurement leads
- Exec sponsor: Priya Sood (CPO) — long-term owner of outcomes.
- Day-to-day champion: Jordan Matthews (Head of ML) — technical owner.
- Procurement contact (please confirm): procurement@atlasai.com

Escalation / risk items to flag for leadership
- Custom IP/data handling needs (NDA / contract clause required).
- SOC 2 + pen-test timeline — may delay SOW if not available within 2 weeks.
- Pricing approval threshold for bespoke single-tenant options.

Attachments we discussed
- SOW-outline.xlsx (outline of pilot scope and commercial model)
- Pilot-success-metrics.docx (suggested KPIs + measurement plan)

Immediate next steps (owner / ETA)
- Redwood: send security pack + SOW draft by Mon 2026-11-09 (Ben)
- AtlasAI: confirm procurement contact and target PO timeline by EOD Fri 2026-11-06 (Jordan/Priya)
- All: 30-min procurement + security sync — propose Tue 2026-11-10 10:00 PT (calendar invite to follow)

Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachment stubs: SOW-outline.xlsx, Pilot-success-metrics.docx

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sabrina Lin <sabrina@stratminds.ai>, Michael Cho <michael.cho@stratminds.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 16:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick recap + asks: deployment topology and migration timing

Thanks for the call earlier — good discussion. I wanted to capture the important bits and lay out immediate next steps so we keep momentum. TL;DR: StratMinds is leaning toward Reserved/Dedicated for predictable production throughput, needs a private option for regulated customers, and has a 2027 Q1 target to cut over critical APIs.

Key notes from discovery:
- Deployment preference: Dedicated capacity for production (predictable TTF/throughput). Hosted API acceptable for dev and early testing. Private VPC considered for regulated pockets.
- Migration source: mixture of an on-prem GPU cluster running a self-hosted endpoint plus intermittent calls to a third-party LLM API for fallback.
- Constraints: data residency (US-only), SSO (SAML + Okta), and HSM/KMS requirement for key material.
- Traffic profile: spike to ~220 reqs/sec during business hours, mostly sub-300 token responses.
- Timeline: pilot in Dec (internal dev), production cutover target Feb–Mar 2027.

Open questions for you (if you can reply inline it helps):
1) Confirm primary production lane (Dedicated vs Private): you said Dedicated, is private only for regulated customers or an across-the-board requirement?
2) Is the on-prem cluster going to be decommissioned at cutover or kept as warm fallback?
3) Any procurement windows or procurement approvers we should engage now (PO lead, legal contact)?
4) Are there any IP/evidence artifacts we need to surface early (SOC 2 reports, encryption diagrams)?

Suggested next steps:
- Sabrina: please share the infra diagram and the short migration answers sheet (we can put on a shared Drive).
- Marissa: can you prepare the Dedicated commitment options and a rough timeline for reserved capacity onboarding?
- Book a 30-min security sync with your SecOps + our security engineer (Jonas) — propose Wed Nov 10 or Mon Nov 15.

Attachments: I attached a one-page migration questionnaire for quick answers.

Thanks — I’ll follow up with a calendar invite once I get the preferred security sync time.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-migration-questionnaire.docx (docx)]

From: Sabrina Lin <sabrina@stratminds.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Cho <michael.cho@stratminds.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 09:21:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick recap + asks: deployment topology and migration timing

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. Quick inline answers below and I’ve attached our infra diagram and the filled migration sheet.

1) Primary production lane: Dedicated is the target for our main public API. Private VPC will be required only for the regulated verticals (finance customers) — those will be cut over later in a separate lift-and-shift.
2) On-prem cluster: we want to decommission the legacy GPU fleet post-cutover but keep it available for 6 weeks as rollback capacity.
3) Procurement: PO lead is Michael Cho (cc’d). Legal: contracts@stratminds.ai. We typically run a 4–6 week procurement cycle once terms are agreed.
4) Evidence: SOC 2 + encryption diagrams will be required for internal audits; SSO/SCIM for user provisioning. We can share a redacted SOC 2 report.

Additional notes:
- Traffic: true peak is closer to 240 reqs/sec during product demos; median is ~45.
- Latency target: p95 < 220ms for inference responses.
- Secret management: we use AWS KMS and would expect a KMS integration or HSM compatibility.

Attached: stratminds-infra-diagram.pdf, stratminds-migration-answers.xlsx

Happy to sync — Nov 10 works for our security lead at 10am PT.

— Sabrina
Sabrina Lin

Subject: Re: Post-demo follow-up: SOC 2 Type II report, bridge letter & trust portal access

Hi Sarah —

Thanks for the questionnaire and the clear list of asks. I'm Irene on Redwood's security team — copying Ben/Marissa so they're aware of timelines. Below are answers and the next steps to get your reviewers access.

Short answers to your three questions:
- Customer-managed keys: We offer integrations with major cloud KMS providers for Dedicated/Private deployments. For Hosted API customers we currently manage KMS; we can provide architecture details and an HSM-backed key option for enterprise contracts.
- Encryption: Data in transit is TLS 1.2+ (TLS 1.3 encouraged). At-rest encryption uses AES-256 for storage volumes and encrypted token caches. We also support application-layer encryption for sensitive fields on request.
- Regions & regulatory scope: Hosted instances persist data in the region chosen at account creation (us-west-2, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1). For HIPAA/GDPR scope: we have a HIPAA program and can sign a BAA; GDPR controls are documented in our DPA. Please flag which datasets you consider PHI so we can confirm scope.

Portal access & artifacts:
- Trust Portal: I can provision a read-only reviewer account for Helium within 1-3 business days. Portal URL: https://trust.redwood.ai/portal/heliumhealth
- Interim documentation: We can provide a bridge letter (temporary attestation) within 24 hours while we confirm access levels.
- SOC 2 Type II report: We typically share the full report via the portal. If you require a one-off PDF sent to a distribution list, we can accommodate after verifying your NDA/role-based access.

Requested items from Helium to speed this up:
1) Confirm reviewer emails to provision (we'll create accounts with least privilege).
2) Confirm whether you need a PDF copy immediately or portal-only.
3) If you need a BAA or DPA draft, we can share our standard template; I'll include a link below.

Timeline:
- Portal invite: 1-3 business days after we receive reviewer emails and your preferred access scope.
- Bridge letter: within 24 hours of confirmation.
- Full report: portal delivery once reviewer accounts are provisioned and access terms agreed.

Links and resources:
- Trust Portal: https://trust.redwood.ai/portal/heliumhealth
- Standard DPA/BAA template: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a2b3c-example-dpa

I've also answered several fields in the attached VSQ where we can respond without an architecture deep-dive. If you want, Ben or Marissa can schedule a short technical review with our infra team.

Best,
Irene Choi
Senior Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

Hi Sarah —

Thanks for the questionnaire and the clear list of asks. I'm Irene on Redwood's security team — copying Ben/Marissa so they're aware of timelines. Below are answers and the next steps to get your reviewers access.

Short answers to your three questions:
- Customer-managed keys: We offer integrations with major cloud KMS providers for Dedicated/Private deployments. For Hosted API customers we currently manage KMS; we can provide architecture details and an HSM-backed key option for enterprise contracts.
- Encryption: Data in transit is TLS 1.2+ (TLS 1.3 encouraged). At-rest encryption uses AES-256 for storage volumes and encrypted token caches. We also support application-layer encryption for sensitive fields on request.
- Regions & regulatory scope: Hosted instances persist data in the region chosen at account creation (us-west-2, eu-west-1, ap-southeast-1). For HIPAA/GDPR scope: we have a HIPAA program and can sign a BAA; GDPR controls are documented in our DPA. Please flag which datasets you consider PHI so we can confirm scope.

Portal access & artifacts:
- Trust Portal: I can provision a read-only reviewer account for Helium within 1-3 business days. Portal URL: https://trust.redwood.ai/portal/heliumhealth
- Interim documentation: We can provide a bridge letter (temporary attestation) within 24 hours while we confirm access levels.
- SOC 2 Type II report: We typically share the full report via the portal. If you require a one-off PDF sent to a distribution list, we can accommodate after verifying your NDA/role-based access.

Requested items from Helium to speed this up:
1) Confirm reviewer emails to provision (we'll create accounts with least privilege).
2) Confirm whether you need a PDF copy immediately or portal-only.
3) If you need a BAA or DPA draft, we can share our standard template; I'll include a link below.

Timeline:
- Portal invite: 1-3 business days after we receive reviewer emails and your preferred access scope.
- Bridge letter: within 24 hours of confirmation.
- Full report: portal delivery once reviewer accounts are provisioned and access terms agreed.

Links and resources:
- Trust Portal: https://trust.redwood.ai/portal/heliumhealth
- Standard DPA/BAA template: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1a2b3c-example-dpa

I've also answered several fields in the attached VSQ where we can respond without an architecture deep-dive. If you want, Ben or Marissa can schedule a short technical review with our infra team.

Best,
Irene Choi
Senior Security Engineer, Redwood Inference

Aisha — thanks for the note.

We've been hitting a cluster of 500/502 responses during our high-concurrency embedding runs. Sample below from yesterday at 15:23 PST: 

[ERROR] 2026-11-01T15:23:07Z - upstream fetch failed - status=502 - request_id=finova-req-3b9
Trace: upstream timeout after 1.2s, then 504 followed by 502 on retry

This spike impacted our pipeline for ~18 minutes and caused about 7% of the batch to fail and retry, which artificially inflated our token costs and blocked a subset of tests. Attaching a trimmed dump we pulled: error_dump_1103.txt.

Given this is a performance-sensitive evaluation and our go/no-go depends on predictable latency under load, we'd like Redwood to treat this as priority support. Elena and I are available for a bridge this afternoon if you can get an engineer on it.

Thanks,
Tom
Director of Platform Engineering
Finova Payments

[Attachment: error_dump_1103.txt]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Tom Harris <tom.harris@finova.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 08:45:30 -0800
Subject: Re: Weekly MAP checkpoint — Finova evaluation & SLA exception

Tom / Aisha —

Thanks for the logs, Tom. I'm kicking off triage now. Early observations: the 502s line up with a short-lived saturation event on the inference pool that correlates to a higher-than-expected prefix-cache churn during your burst tests. Hypothesis: a combination of cache thrash + an autoscaler throttle edge-case that delayed pod warmups under sudden concurrency.

What I'll do within the next 24 hours:
- Pull shard-level metrics and correlated pod lifecycle events (owner: Vivek)
- Replay the exact request pattern in a controlled staging environment to reproduce (owner: Vivek)
- Provide a mitigation playbook: short-term (rate-limit recommendation + temporary capacity bump) and medium-term (cache tuning + autoscaler config)

Immediate ask from Finova: could you share the exact request concurrency profile (RPS and batch sizes) and any synthetic test scripts so we can reproduce? If you can paste a small excerpt of the traffic pattern, that would speed up root cause.

Tentative ETA: preliminary triage findings by EOD 11/04; mitigation steps and timeline by 11/05. If we reproduce and confirm infra-related root cause, I'll recommend a temporary capacity uplift for your Dedicated eval window to stabilize latency while we push the autoscaler fix.

I'll be on a call at 11:30 — otherwise, available on Slack.

Thanks,
Vivek
Staff Engineer, Runtime Reliability
vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com
