title: Re: HelioScale — pricing parity & transition path
preview:
From: Victor Huang <victor.h@helioscale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Patel <maya.patel@helioscale.com>
Date: Sun, 01 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: HelioScale — pricing parity request & transition support

Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. HelioScale is evaluating two providers for our inference layer replacement and ModelServeX has provided an aggressive price deck that matches our current unit economics. Before we move forward with commercial talks I wanted to see whether Redwood could:

- Match ModelServeX's effective blended price for text-gen + embeddings across our 12M token baseline
- Provide a migration credit or one-time onboarding credit to offset our termination costs with the incumbent
- Commit to a 99.9% latency SLA on our live traffic profile (p95 target < 250ms)

Attached is a short RFP-like spreadsheet with our usage slices and current blended price. Can you share a concrete counter-offer and what packaging/commitments you would need to get to parity?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — our procurement window closes mid-November.

Best,
Victor Huang
Head of Platform Partnerships
HelioScale

[attachment: HelioScale_RFP_response_v3.xlsx]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Victor Huang <victor.h@helioscale.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 08:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: HelioScale — pricing parity request & transition support

Victor —

Thanks for the clarity and the RFP. Quick read-through: we can absolutely approach price parity on committed terms, and we have a couple of levers to make the transition economically attractive. High-level reply here, Rishi/Laura looping for delivery and legal input.

What we can do now (initial offer):
- 24-month committed term at a blended effective token rate that targets parity for your 12M baseline (details below)
- One-time migration credit equal to 25% of estimated remaining term penalties (we'll need an invoice/statement)
- Free onboarding runbook + one week remote pairing with our platform engineers
- SLA: 99.9% availability; for latency SLOs we can include p95 targets and a performance credit schedule in the SOW

Open items we need from you:
- Confirmation of current baseline token mix (short/medium/long seq) by tomorrow
- Whether you require on-net egress allowances or will accept standard egress terms

I'll attach a drafted commercial term sheet once Rishi confirms the blended rates.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 10:40:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: HelioScale pricing parity — draft numbers

Ben/Laura —

Quick internal note before I send the term sheet to Victor: middle-case blended pricing to match ModelServeX comes to an effective $0.0009 per token (blended across gen+embedding slices) with a 24-month commit and standard reserved capacity assumptions. If we remove the 24-month commit and go to 12 months, price moves to ~$0.0012 per token.

Couple of product constraints: the 25% migration credit is okay but needs a cap (propose cap at $150k), and onboarding pairing is time-boxed to 5 engineer-days. We can include an optional 3-month free prefix cache period (reduces early token cost) but that requires legal signoff on data retention windows.

I put notes in the draft term sheet and flagged the egress ask — we can offer a 50TB complimentary egress bucket for the first 90 days if they sign the 24-month.

Sending the draft to Ben to share.

-Rishi
Head of Deals, Redwood Inference

[attachment: migration_runbook_draft.docx]

From: Victor Huang <victor.h@helioscale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Patel <maya.patel@helioscale.com>
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 11:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HelioScale — pricing parity & transition support

Ben/Rishi —

Thanks for the quick response. A few clarifying points from our side:
- We can stretch to a 24-month commitment if there are robust migration protections and an initial usage credit to cover the first two months as we validate inference quality.
- The incumbent has an early termination estimate of $420k; can Redwood's 25% migration credit be applied against that and be invoiced or netted at contract execution?
- Egress: we will need at least 100TB of free egress for the first 60 days given dataset sizes for model tuning.

If you can get closer on the migration credit cap and extend the egress to 100TB, we can push this to procurement for a signed LOI next week.

Best,
Victor


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Victor Huang <victor.h@helioscale.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio_alvarez@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: HelioScale — pricing parity & transition support

Victor —

Good questions. After checking with Rishi and legal:
- Migration credit: we can cover 25% of the documented early termination up to a cap of $200k. The credit will be applied as an invoice credit at onboarding (netted against first invoice). We'll need a statement of charges from your incumbent to validate.
- Egress: we can extend complimentary egress to 100TB for the first 60 days on the 24-month commit.
- Free prefix cache period: we'll include a 90-day prefix/KV caching boost to lower your initial token bill while you warm caches; this is part of the migration package.
- SLA/latency: we'll include p95 latency targets and a three-tier performance credit schedule in the SOW; latency credits apply only if we fail the p95 SLO for two consecutive weeks.

I'll share a redlined commercial term sheet (PDF) this afternoon and a DocuSign link for a preliminary LOI that captures the commit + migration credit + egress. Laura, Sergio — please review the redlines for billing language and credits.

Thanks,
Ben

[attachment: HelioScale_counter_offer_redlines.pdf]

From: Maya Patel <maya.patel@helioscale.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Victor Huang <victor.h@helioscale.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 05 Nov 2026 16:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: HelioScale — pricing parity & transition support

Ben/Victor —

Quick confirmation from procurement: the 24-month term + $200k migration credit (applied as invoice credit) + 100TB egress for 60 days is acceptable as a starting commercial position. Please push the LOI DocuSign link and the term sheet PDF; procurement will attach the incumbent termination statement and circle back with legal for final MSA redlines.

Next steps we expect:
- Redwood sends LOI via DocuSign (we sign)
- HelioScale provides incumbent termination statement within 48 hours of LOI execution
- Redwood issues onboarding schedule and assigns engineers for the two-week migration window

Thanks —
Maya Patel
Procurement Manager
HelioScale

[DocuSign link requested]

title: Remittance rhythm & conditional credit cap for Helix pilot
preview:
From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@helixtx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Trevor Ames <trevor.ames@helixtx.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: Move to Net60 -> Net45 + temporary credit increase (pilot expansion)

Ben —

Thanks again for the kickoff yesterday. Quick ask from our finance team: as we expand the pilot to two additional teams, we will exceed the Pilot credit band you quoted. Can Redwood temporarily increase our credit cap by $75k while we move to a PO-backed contract?

We currently operate on Net60 with a monthly invoice cadence. To line up with our internal disbursements, we would prefer Net45 for the next 90 days (covers the expansion runway). If Redwood can accept Net45 conditional on a 25% prepayment or an onboarding deposit, that would help us move faster.

Also: for EU usage, we will need invoices that support reverse-charge — we’ll provide VAT IDs for the EU entities involved. Attached is our proposed remittance schedule (Helix_Payment_Schedule_v2.xlsx).

Can you confirm whether Redwood can:
- Approve a temporary $75k credit increase (subject to PO)
- Move to Net45 for 90 days with a 25% deposit
- Issue invoices split by billing entity for VAT reverse-charge

Appreciate a quick read — Trevor and I are hoping to finalize this by end of week so we can start the second pilot cohort next Monday.

Best,
Laura Chen
Head of Procurement
Helix Therapeutics

Attachment: Helix_Payment_Schedule_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet, 28KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Laura Chen <laura.chen@helixtx.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: Move to Net60 -> Net45 + temporary credit increase (pilot expansion)

Laura — thanks for the clear ask and for the schedule. Read through the attachment and talked this over with finance. High level: we can get you runway but with a couple of guardrails so risk is managed on both sides. Proposed compromise below — happy to jump on a 15-min call to lock this down.

Proposal (Redwood):
- Temporary credit increase: +$75k conditional on receipt of an executed PO and basic KYC docs (company reg and AP contact). Credit increase effective upon PO date and expires after 90 days unless extended by mutual agreement.
- Payment terms: Net45 for invoices dated during the 90-day expansion window.
- Upfront: 25% onboarding deposit to be wired within 7 business days of PO to avoid interruption; remaining invoiced Net45.
- Invoicing & VAT: We’ll issue invoices per billing entity. Kimberly, can you confirm tax-side requirements for the EU reverse-charge flow (what exact evidence we need)?
- Start date: Redwood will schedule the second cohort start once deposit is received and PO accepted.

Next steps: Laura — if that works, send over the PO and AP contact and we’ll route to finance/legal for quick execution. I’ll thread in our tax lead (Kimberly) and finance (Evelyn) — Kim/Evelyn: can you confirm whether we need a short SOW amendment and DocuSign for the credit condition?

Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0142

Subject: Re: Request: Move to Net60 -> Net45 + temporary credit increase (pilot expansion)

Laura / Ben —

Thanks for looping me in. Quick tax/legal points so everyone is aligned:

1) VAT reverse-charge (EU): we will need the customer VAT ID for each EU billing entity and a statement on the invoice indicating reverse-charge applies (we use EU VAT directive language). A purchase order that references the correct billing legal entity is sufficient for our routing. Please provide VAT IDs and the legal entity names.

2) SOW / amendment: because the credit cap change is temporary and conditional on a PO, we typically execute a one-paragraph SOW amendment that attaches the PO and confirms the 25% deposit and 90-day window. We can do this via DocuSign (I’ll create a short amendment template and attach a DocuSign link).

3) Attachments and audit trail: please upload the PO and any AP remittance instructions to the HubSpot record (I included the link below). Evelyn, from finance: once we have PO + deposit + VAT IDs, can we provision the credit increase?

Link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/record/HLX-4682
DocuSign (will create envelope): https://www.docusign.com/envelope/ABC123

Ben — I’ll draft the amendment and the minimum VAT wording required. Laura — if you can send VAT IDs and the PO, I’ll kick off DocuSign and loop legal for a 1-business-day review.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Sr. Counsel, Commercial & Tax
Redwood Inference

Hi Dana — thanks for the clear template and the list of requirements. Appreciate the level of detail. Quick answers and proposals below.

1) PO header fields: yes — Redwood can include PO number, cost center (custom field), remit address, EIN and contract reference on invoice line items. We will need the exact cost center format (string vs numeric) you use for ERP matching; can you send an example of the field value?

2) Invoice cadence options:
- Standard Redwood approach: monthly consolidated invoice with line-level PO reference (default, easiest for accounting).
- Weekly consolidated invoice: we can support weekly runs but it requires a slight change to our billing pipeline (forcing earlier cutoffs) — operationally doable but adds a small per-invoice processing fee.

3) Net terms: Redwood standard is Net30. For large strategic customers we evaluate Net45/Net60 with commercial approval and an associated discount or prepay arrangement to offset receivables. Proposal: if Catalyst can commit to a 12-month minimum ARR threshold (we can align on number in the commercial doc), we can offer Net45 at standard list rates or Net60 with a 1.5% service-charge/discount tradeoff. If you prefer no extra fees, Net45 would be the path we can approve faster.

4) Invoice routing: please confirm AP inbox; we can send invoices to a dedicated AP mailbox and cc your procurement contact.

I've attached a short Redwood_PO_Guidance.pdf that maps the PO fields we can populate and sample line-item formatting to ease your ERP matching. If it's helpful, I can connect your AP lead with our finance ops engineer for a short 30-minute mapping call.

Next steps I propose:
- You confirm cost center sample and AP email
- I loop in Vanessa from finance for approval on Net45/Net60 and any discount tier we negotiate

Happy to hop on a call if that speeds things up.

Best,
Ben Carter

title: CloudPO overage guardrails & channel revenue shielding (routing options)
preview:
From: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 1 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Hi Ben,

Quick question as we're finalizing the PO: our procurement team is pushing for explicit overage guardrails on the private offer so we don't get surprise charges during the pilot period. Can you confirm whether Redwood can: 

- Cap overage billing to a percentage of committed spend per month (e.g., +20% cap)
- Emit forecasted usage alerts 48 hours before spikes so our SREs can throttle

Also need clarity on how channel revenue is routed when we buy via the marketplace — will the reseller commission be deducted from the invoice or handled as a separate pass-through?

We're aiming to sign by 11/12 if terms are clear. Attaching our standard PO terms for reference.

Thanks,
Sam Turner
Procurement Manager, CloudBuyer
procurement@brightlane.com

Attachment: BrightLane_PO_Terms.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Hi Sam — thanks for the clear ask and timeline. Looping in Irene (ops/legal) for the routing piece; copying Jordan for deal ops. 

Short answers up front:
- Overage cap: we can offer a temporary pilot cap (example: 20% of monthly committed during first 90 days) as a bill-protection clause, subject to renewal terms.
- Forecast alerts: yes — we can push quota/usage webhooks and e-mails 48 hours prior; we will work with your SRE to configure thresholds.

Two channel-routing options we typically propose (pros/cons below):
1) Marketplace pass-through: Redwood invoices BrightLane for list after marketplace takes reseller cut. Pros: cleaner for marketplace accounting. Cons: reseller cut reflected as a reduction on invoice; longer reconciliation.
2) Direct billing + reseller rebate: Redwood invoices BrightLane at net price and issues a separate rebate payment to reseller. Pros: keeps invoice net and predictable; Cons: requires rebate mechanics & partner bookkeeping.

I'm attaching a one-page summary (PO_Term_Summary.xlsx) with the two options and sample numbers. Request: Irene, can you confirm whether legal is comfortable with a short-term 20% cap and which routing approach is preferable from a compliance standpoint?

Next step: if we agree on approach, Jordan can produce a mutual action plan and we can lock dates for the DocuSign.

Best,
Ben Carter
Head of Marketplace Deals — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: PO_Term_Summary.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Ben / Jordan / Sam — thanks, good framing. A few operational constraints and a recommended safe path: 

Constraints:
- For marketplace pass-through (option 1), AWS/GCP billing systems force the reseller cut to appear before Redwood's net revenue is recorded — we cannot present a separate rebate on the same invoice. That can complicate revenue recognition for BrightLane depending on your AR processes.
- Direct billing + rebate (option 2) is legally simpler for Redwood (we own the invoicing relationship) but requires an explicit rebate agreement with the reseller and a reconciliation cadence.

Recommended path for pilots:
- Use a temporary 20% monthly overage cap (pilot only) as a contract addendum. Include a roll-off clause after 90 days that defaults to 0% cap unless both parties agree.
- Opt for Direct billing + reseller rebate for the pilot: keeps BrightLane's invoice stable and avoids marketplace billing delays. We can run the rebate as a monthly netting entry to the reseller.

Action items:
- I will draft the addendum language for the 20% cap and a rebate clause (will attach in next email).
- Jordan: please build the mutual action plan with milestone: addendum sign (3 business days), PO issuance (within 5 days).

Irene Choi
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>, Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 10:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Adding the revenue modelling side — Sam/Jordan/Ben/Irene, I ran three simple scenarios to show net cash flows and reseller treatment so you can pick the cleanest option for BrightLane's finance team. Attached CSV has month-by-month examples. 

Summary (numbers illustrative):
- Scenario A (Marketplace pass-through): BrightLane sees list price on invoice, reseller cut 12% deducted at source. Redwood records net revenue after cut. Result: BrightLane must account for reseller deduction; timing lag 30-45 days.
- Scenario B (Direct billing + rebate): BrightLane is invoiced at agreed net price; Redwood pays reseller a monthly rebate. Result: Invoice simplicity for BrightLane; Redwood maintains rebate accruals.
- Scenario C (Hybrid with escrow for overages): Net billing for committed usage, any overage above 20% held in escrow pending uplift review. This reduces immediate cash flow risk but adds reconciliation work.

Recommendation: For a short 90-day pilot, Scenario B (Direct billing + rebate) minimizes surprise for BrightLane and supports a faster procurement sign-off. If procurement requires marketplace-native invoicing for audit trail, we can adopt Scenario A with a short reconciliation schedule.

Please see attached revshield_scenarios.csv and flag which dataset you want expanded into a full month-by-month cashflow model.

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Senior Revenue Ops Analyst — Redwood Inference

Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>, Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 10:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Adding the revenue modelling side — Sam/Jordan/Ben/Irene, I ran three simple scenarios to show net cash flows and reseller treatment so you can pick the cleanest option for BrightLane's finance team. Attached CSV has month-by-month examples. 

Summary (numbers illustrative):
- Scenario A (Marketplace pass-through): BrightLane sees list price on invoice, reseller cut 12% deducted at source. Redwood records net revenue after cut. Result: BrightLane must account for reseller deduction; timing lag 30-45 days.
- Scenario B (Direct billing + rebate): BrightLane is invoiced at agreed net price; Redwood pays reseller a monthly rebate. Result: Invoice simplicity for BrightLane; Redwood maintains rebate accruals.
- Scenario C (Hybrid with escrow for overages): Net billing for committed usage, any overage above 20% held in escrow pending uplift review. This reduces immediate cash flow risk but adds reconciliation work.

Recommendation: For a short 90-day pilot, Scenario B (Direct billing + rebate) minimizes surprise for BrightLane and supports a faster procurement sign-off. If procurement requires marketplace-native invoicing for audit trail, we can adopt Scenario A with a short reconciliation schedule.

Please see attached revshield_scenarios.csv and flag which dataset you want expanded into a full month-by-month cashflow model.

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Senior Revenue Ops Analyst — Redwood Inference

title: MetroLine: priority uptime & latency commitment — approval path and action log
preview:
From: Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Hi Soojin,

Thanks again for the demo last week — MetroLine is excited about Redwood's routing and caching capabilities. As we move toward procurement, our platform product team has asked whether Redwood can commit to a sub-100ms p95 for text generation calls tied to our Premium tier (traffic profile attached). We know this is an exception to standard offers, so we want to surface the ask early:

- Required: p95 < 100ms for our steady-state traffic (avg 6 qps per tenant, burst to 120 qps across region).
- Expected term: 12 months, with a 6-month ramp.
- Region: us-west + us-east.
- Procurement ask: outline any dedicated capacity needs and an indicative uplift to list pricing.

Attached: MetroLine_SLA_Request_v1.pdf (traffic profile + business justification).

Can you confirm feasibility and a rough timeline to get an internal approval path? If feasible we can loop in contracts (cc'd) and share a target DocuSign window.

Best,
Elaine Park
Head of Platform, MetroLine

Attachments: MetroLine_SLA_Request_v1.pdf

From: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>, contracts@metroline.io
Date: 2026-11-01T12:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Team — looping you all in. High-level notes up front: this is a high-value strategic account; we should treat as an exception but keep guardrails. Initial product/infra observations below.

1) Feasibility (quick take)
- Achievable with a dedicated capacity reservation per region + strict routing to small-seq models or optimized quantized variants.
- Need changes: lower KV cache TTL for MetroLine prefixes, pre-warm KV cache on expected sessions, and guaranteed local batch windows (to avoid cross-tenant queuing).

2) Operational asks
- Canary policy + minute-level telemetry for p95 and backlog.
- Runbook and automatic fallback to a higher-latency backstop model (with clear user-facing messaging).

3) Commercials (ballpark)
- Expect ~25–35% uplift on hosted list depending on reserved pool size. Dedicated capacity reduces variance but increases committed cost.

Next steps (ask list):
- Aditya: feasibility + timeline and whether we need a code path or config-only change.
- Marcus: pricing recommendation and contract language.
- Monica: CS/ops runbook & escalation path coordination.
- Laura: leadership signoff path for an SLA carveout (if needed).

Attaching a quick cost stub: initial-cost-estimate.xlsx (very rough).

Soojin Lee
Product Ops, Redwood Inference

Attachments: initial-cost-estimate.xlsx

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Thanks for the context and the traffic profile. I read the attachment. Short technical readout:

- Root cause of p95 pressure is a mix of bursty concurrent sessions and long-context requests that increase KV cache misses.
- Changes needed: (a) route MetroLine traffic to model variants with short-context optimized kernels, (b) implement prefix-level proactive KV prewarm for known sessions, (c) enforce per-tenant batch caps to limit batch build-up.

Effort estimate:
- Config + policy changes (routing, TTL) = ~2 weeks (config + canary).
- KV prewarm + telemetry hooks = ~4–6 weeks.
- Kernel/exec optimization for specific model variant (if required) = ~8–12 weeks.

Risk & mitigations:
- If we only do config changes, we can hit 80–90% of the target in week 2–3 but may still see outlier p95s under extreme bursts. Recommend committing to a dedicated pool for the first 3 months while we roll in prewarm and telemetry.

Operational requirements:
- A runbook with explicit canary traffic pattern, rollback steps, and SLA credit calculation inputs.
- SRE involvement for on-call paging and eviction rules during the initial ramp.

I'll draft a short implementation plan and a minimal gating checklist by EOD Wednesday.

—Aditya
Sr. Engineer, Serving Runtime

On Mon, Nov 1, 2026 at 12:04 PM Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team — looping you all in. High-level notes up front: this is a high-value strategic account; we should treat as an exception but keep guardrails. Initial product/infra observations below.

Attachments referenced: initial-cost-estimate.xlsx

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, contracts@metroline.io, Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>
Date: 2026-11-03T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

High-level commercial proposal:

1) Commitment model
- 12 month term with a 6-month ramp; minimum committed spend to cover a dedicated pool (per-region).
- Pricing uplift: 20% base uplift + a usage-weighted premium for dedicated overhead (final range 20–30%).

2) SLA language (draft clause)
- p95 latency target: 100ms. Credits: 10% pro-rata credit for monthly p95 between 100–140ms; 25% credit if p95 > 140ms for two consecutive weeks.
- Exceptions: planned maintenance windows, force majeure, and customer load spikes beyond agreed burst profile.

3) Governance & limits
- SLA carveout limited to 3 named MetroLine production clusters/regions (us-west, us-east).
- Auto-fallback to a functional but higher-latency model if we detect sustained backlog beyond 5 minutes.

I attached a short draft of the term language (draft_term_sla.md) — please review. Marcus suggests we push for a 20% uplift as the baseline and reserve a negotiation window for the customer.

Marcus Lin
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference

Attachments: draft_term_sla.md

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>, contracts@metroline.io
Date: 2026-11-04T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Approved in principle with the following conditions:

- Must deliver an operations runbook (use runbook_template.md) with clear canary test patterns, rollback procedures, and a named escalation chain before the SLA is signed.
- We limit the carveout to the two named regions only and a maximum of three production clusters. No global rollouts without separate signoff.
- A canary of at least 48 hours with synthetic traffic and verification hooks in Console (latency, queue length, token throughput).
- Contract credits and the maintenance exclusion in Marcus's draft look reasonable; legal should tighten the exception language.

Action items (owners):
- Aditya: implementation plan + gating checklist by 2026-11-10.
- Monica: ops runbook draft and on-call pager assignment by 2026-11-09.
- Marcus: final pricing sheet and updated draft_term_sla.md with clearer credit calculation by 2026-11-06.
- Soojin: consolidate and share a one-pager for MetroLine and start the DocuSign flow once the three items above are complete.

I authorize proceeding on those conditions. Keep the MetroLine execs informed but do not expand the carveout beyond named clusters.

Laura Bennett
VP, Product & GTM, Redwood Inference

> ...

Thanks all — summary and immediate next steps below. Elaine: sharing this back to keep you aligned with our internal path.

Summary decisions:

Subject: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Hi Soojin,

Thanks again for the demo last week — MetroLine is excited about Redwood's routing and caching capabilities. As we move toward procurement, our platform product team has asked whether Redwood can commit to a sub-100ms p95 for text generation calls tied to our Premium tier (traffic profile attached). We know this is an exception to standard offers, so we want to surface the ask early:

- Required: p95 < 100ms for our steady-state traffic (avg 6 qps per tenant, burst to 120 qps across region).
- Expected term: 12 months, with a 6-month ramp.
- Region: us-west + us-east.
- Procurement ask: outline any dedicated capacity needs and an indicative uplift to list pricing.

Attached: MetroLine_SLA_Request_v1.pdf (traffic profile + business justification).

Can you confirm feasibility and a rough timeline to get an internal approval path? If feasible we can loop in contracts (cc'd) and share a target DocuSign window.

Best,
Elaine Park
Head of Platform, MetroLine

Attachments: MetroLine_SLA_Request_v1.pdf

From: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>

Date: 2026-11-01T12:04:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Team — looping you all in. High-level notes up front: this is a high-value strategic account; we should treat as an exception but keep guardrails. Initial product/infra observations below.

1) Feasibility (quick take)
- Achievable with a dedicated capacity reservation per region + strict routing to small-seq models or optimized quantized variants.
- Need changes: lower KV cache TTL for MetroLine prefixes, pre-warm KV cache on expected sessions, and guaranteed local batch windows (to avoid cross-tenant queuing).

2) Operational asks
- Canary policy + minute-level telemetry for p95 and backlog.
- Runbook and automatic fallback to a higher-latency backstop model (with clear user-facing messaging).

3) Commercials (ballpark)
- Expect ~25–35% uplift on hosted list depending on reserved pool size. Dedicated capacity reduces variance but increases committed cost.

Next steps (ask list):
- Aditya: feasibility + timeline and whether we need a code path or config-only change.
- Marcus: pricing recommendation and contract language.
- Monica: CS/ops runbook & escalation path coordination.
- Laura: leadership signoff path for an SLA carveout (if needed).

Attaching a quick cost stub: initial-cost-estimate.xlsx (very rough).

Soojin Lee
Product Ops, Redwood Inference

Attachments: initial-cost-estimate.xlsx

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin_lee@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Elaine Park <elaine@metroline.io>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sub-100ms p95 for MetroLine Premium (SLA ask)

Thanks for the context and the traffic profile. I read the attachment. Short technical readout:

- Root cause of p95 pressure is a mix of bursty concurrent sessions and long-context requests that increase KV cache misses.
- Changes needed: (a) route MetroLine traffic to model variants with short-context optimized kernels, (b) implement prefix-level proactive KV prewarm for known sessions, (c) enforce per-tenant batch caps to limit batch build-up.

Effort estimate:
- Config + policy changes (routing, TTL) = ~2 weeks (config + canary).
- KV prewarm + telemetry hooks = ~4–6 weeks.
- Kernel/exec optimization for specific model variant (if required) = ~8–12 weeks.

Risk & mitigations:
- If we only do config changes, we can hit 80–90% of the target in week 2–3 but may still see outlier p95s under extreme bursts. Recommend committing to a dedicated pool for the first 3 months while we roll in prewarm and telemetry.

Operational requirements:
- A runbook with explicit canary traffic pattern, rollback steps, and SLA credit calculation inputs.
- SRE involvement for on-call paging and eviction rules during the initial ramp.

I'll draft a short implementation plan and a minimal gating checklist by EOD Wednesday.

—Aditya
Sr. Engineer, Serving Runtime

2) SLA language (draft clause)
- p95 latency target: 100ms. Credits: 10% pro-rata credit for monthly p95 between 100–140ms; 25% credit if p95 > 140ms for two consecutive weeks.

3) Governance & limits

- Auto-fallback to a functional but higher-latency model if we detect sustained backlog beyond 5 minutes.

title: Request: allocation & calculation for partial degradation on Pegasus network
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Pegasus Platform Ops <ops@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Hi Ben,

We observed an increase in 5xx rates and tail latency for your account's inference endpoints between 08:15 and 08:57 PT this morning while Pegasus performed a rolling network maintenance affecting our east-us backbone. Our initial aggregation shows ~42 minutes of partial degradation (elevated latency and ~2.4% 5xx). I'm attaching our outage log (pegasus-outage-log.csv).

Per our standard process, we'd like Redwood to confirm the impact on your side and advise if you're requesting a credit under the SLA. If you can share your request and any evidence, we'll route it to billing.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Senior Support Lead
Pegasus Cloud

Attachment: pegasus-outage-log.csv (text/csv, 23 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Elena,

Thanks for the heads up and for the log. We've pulled our telemetry for the same window. Key notes:
- Redwood's edge metrics show a single 5xx spike from 08:22–08:27 (approx 5 minutes); host-level latency had several 95th-percentile blips between 08:18–08:30 but aggregated token-level latency across RPCs stayed within SLA bands for ~95% of traffic.
- Our automated prefix-KV cache shows no eviction events correlated with the window. We did see increased TCP retransmits originating in the Pegasus network segments (matching your log).

We will attach our internal response timeline and a snippet of the request-level latencies for the period (redwood-response-timeline.pdf). Based on a preliminary read, Redwood believes the majority of the 42-minute window is attributable to Pegasus' maintenance (third-party). Per SLA section 5.2 (Provider outages vs. Third-Party dependency), we dispute a full-credit allocation and request credit apportionment tied to traffic-level impact.

Proposed ask: Pegasus to propose a quantified credit for the portion you attribute to your network (we'll provide our per-route delta to compute proportional credit). Can you confirm the exact start/end timestamps you used and whether internal retries were suppressed by your maintenance policy?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-response-timeline.pdf (application/pdf, 56 KB)

From: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Pegasus Platform Ops <ops@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T08:37:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Ben,

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the timeline. Our start/end timestamps were 08:15:00–08:57:00 PT and were derived from BGP flap and interface counters across the affected PoPs; I've included the raw csv lines in the attached pegasus-outage-log.csv. During the maintenance we intentionally blackholed a subset of transit paths which caused retries from some downstream overlays — we do classify this as a planned maintenance but did not mark it as an exclusion in the contract (we accept the classification as third-party influenced partial degradation).

Given your telemetry shows a significantly shorter error spike, we are open to a proportional credit. Pegasus prelim credit calc (subject to verification) is 30% of one day's service fee for the impacted capacity band. We can finalize after Redwood provides the per-route degraded traffic volume and confirms whether customer retry behaviour inflated observed token counts.

Regards,
Elena Soto
Senior Support Lead
Pegasus Cloud

Attachment: pegasus-outage-log.csv (text/csv, 23 KB)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-03T10:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Elena,

Appreciate the transparency. To move this to billing we need:
1) A time-aligned CSV from Pegasus (you've provided one; can you include the exact UTC offsets and confirm server vs. client timestamps?)
2) Our per-route degraded volume (we'll provide this by EOD)
3) A proposed credit calc showing the numerator (minutes of impact * capacity band) and denominator used

For the SLA review, we rely on section 5.2 and the Appendix A latency bands. Given the mismatch in observed windows, our internal recommendation is a split credit: Pegasus credit applied against network-related minutes, Redwood to cover application-layer retries attributable to our client-side error handling. This prevents double-crediting and keeps the reconciliation simple.

Marissa: can you review the attached SLA excerpt and approve proceeding with a split allocation? If approved, we'll produce our per-route delta CSV and upload both documents to the shared Drive link and DocuSign the amendment for the credit.

Links:
- SLA excerpt: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B3cExample
- DocuSign stub: https://app.docusign.com/contract/ABC123

Attachment: sla-contract.pdf (application/pdf, 75 KB)

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Director, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-04T17:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

All,

I've reviewed the evidence provided. Approve proceeding with the split credit approach. Marcus — please finalize Redwood's per-route delta and upload it to the Drive link. Elena — once we have the matched CSVs we'll accept a proportional credit; please provide your formal calculation and a credit memo we can apply to the account.

Let's aim to close this by 2026-11-12.

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Success
Redwood Inference

title: Capacity add-on & throughput guarantees for Sapphire renewal
preview:
From: Jordan Miles <jmiles@sapphire.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@sapphire.ai>
Date: 2026-11-01T08:15:00-07:00
Subject: Capacity planning: Renewal + required GPUs for Q1 expansion

Ben —

Quick note to kick off our renewal conversation. Our usage has been ramping faster than expected after the new multimodal feature launch. Attached is a snapshot of our last 90 days (sapphire_current_usage.csv) — you'll see a steady upward trend in QPS and tokens per request.

Key asks from our side:
- Can Redwood support a committed pool that guarantees throughput up to 300 QPS (95th) for our production model?
- We want recommended GPU sizing for the renewal (12mo term) with a plan to expand if growth continues.
- Pricing/credit structure if we hit bursts beyond commit.

Our current contract expires 2027-01-31 and we'd like to finalize terms by mid-December so engineering can plan infra changes.

Thanks — can you loop in the right folks for a sizing recommendation?

—Jordan
Jordan Miles
Head of Platform Engineering, Sapphire AI
ops@sapphire.ai
Attachment: sapphire_current_usage.csv (csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Miles <jmiles@sapphire.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Capacity planning: Renewal + required GPUs for Q1 expansion

Jordan —

Thanks for the usage snapshot, that was very helpful. First pass below; Aisha will follow up with technical sizing detail. High-level summary and proposal:

Summary of Sapphire current baseline (from your CSV):
- 90-day average QPS: ~48 req/s
- 95th percentile QPS peak: ~125 req/s
- Avg tokens per request (inference output + prompt): ~820 tokens
- Current Dedicated pool: 6 x A100-80GB reserved (non-auto-scale)

Projection & ask from Sapphire: ~2.4x traffic growth over next 12 months if pipeline converts

Redwood recommended reserved configuration for renewal (12-month term):
- Primary reserved pool: 12 x H100-80GB equivalent GPUs (preferred)
- Burst/buffer capacity: +4 GPUs available for short bursts (autoscale with burst credits)
- Latency SLO to target: 95th <= 650ms for standard generation workload

Sizing rationale (short form):
- We estimate each H100 in your workload (with our batching + KV prefix caching) can sustain ~20-30 sustained QPS for the model profile you shared while keeping 95th tail latency in target range.
- 12 GPUs provides headroom for steady-state projected growth (~2.4x) and leaves burst pool to handle flash spikes up to ~300 QPS at 95th.
- If you prefer A100 hardware we recommend 16 x A100-80GB to reach comparable headroom.

Options to reduce committed count: caching/quantization plan and targeted batching could reduce committed GPUs by ~15% (Aisha will quantify).

Next steps I propose:
1) Aisha to run a short sizing sim with your CSV + model config and produce per-GPU RPS numbers and a final recommended count.
2) Stephanie to model pricing for 12 H100 reserved + burst and a 12-month vs 24-month commit with discount scenarios.
3) Quick sync early next week to lock the renewal window.

I attached an initial calc sheet (sapphire_sizing_calcs.xlsx) for transparency.

If that aligns with you, we'll produce a formal SOW with GPU counts and SLAs.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
Attachment: sapphire_sizing_calcs.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jordan Miles <jmiles@sapphire.ai>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Capacity planning: Renewal + required GPUs for Q1 expansion

Hi Jordan —

I ran a quick simulation against the CSV you provided and your model config (context length ~2k tokens, avg output length 300 tokens). Key technical notes:

- Baseline throughput assumptions: without batching, a single H100 with our optimized execution path gives ~18-22 RPS for your average request size. With moderate batching (target batch sizes ~8-12) that rises to ~28-35 RPS.
- Cache impact: enabling prefix/KV cache for repeated prefixes (common in your flows) reduces effective token compute by ~12-18% which translates to ~10% fewer GPUs for steady-state.
- Quantization/compilation: moving to a 4-bit quantized path for non-critical models can yield another ~8-12% perf gain.

Sizing calc (conservative):
- Projected 12-month avg QPS: 115 req/s -> steady RPS requirement ~115
- Projected 12-month 95th: 300 req/s -> burst/peak handling requirement

Reserve recommendation (final technical):
- Committed pool: 12 x H100 (configured with continuous batching + KV cache) -> sustained capacity ~12 * 30 RPS = ~360 RPS effective (with batching); this covers projected avg+buffer.
- Burst policy: up to +4 GPUs (autoscale) to handle sudden surge to ~300 QPS with headroom.
- If you cannot move to H100, 16 x A100-80GB with same policy is the alternative.

I'll package an itemized sizing tab with the per-GPU RPS numbers and the assumptions (batch sizes, token mix, percent cache hit) and share in the SOW.

If you want, I can also run a 24–48 hour stress test in a dedicated staging pool to validate the numbers before the renewal is signed.

—Aisha

Summary of Sapphire current baseline (from your CSV):
- 90-day average QPS: ~48 req/s
- 95th percentile QPS peak: ~125 req/s

- Burst/buffer capacity: +4 GPUs available for short bursts (autoscale with burst credits)
- Latency SLO to target: 95th <= 650ms for standard generation workload

- We estimate each H100 in your workload (with our batching + KV prefix caching) can sustain ~20-30 sustained QPS for the model profile you shared while keeping 95th tail latency in target range.
- 12 GPUs provides headroom for steady-state projected growth (~2.4x) and leaves burst pool to handle flash spikes up to ~300 QPS at 95th.
- If you prefer A100 hardware we recommend 16 x A100-80GB to reach comparable headroom.

Options to reduce committed count: caching/quantization plan and targeted batching could reduce committed GPUs by ~15% (Aisha will quantify).

- Quantization/compilation: moving to a 4-bit quantized path for non-critical models can yield another ~8-12% perf gain.

Sizing calc (conservative):

- Projected 12-month 95th: 300 req/s -> burst/peak handling requirement

Reserve recommendation (final technical):
- Committed pool: 12 x H100 (configured with continuous batching + KV cache) -> sustained capacity ~12 * 30 RPS = ~360 RPS effective (with batching); this covers projected avg+buffer.
- Burst policy: up to +4 GPUs (autoscale) to handle sudden surge to ~300 QPS with headroom.
- If you cannot move to H100, 16 x A100-80GB with same policy is the alternative.

I'll package an itemized sizing tab with the per-GPU RPS numbers and the assumptions (batch sizes, token mix, percent cache hit) and share in the SOW.

title: PO exception request — pilot onboarding and thresholds
preview:
From: Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finovate.com
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 08:47:12 -0700
Subject: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the demo last week — excited to move the pilot forward. We have a short internal procurement note: our standard AP team prefers issuing a PO for all vendor spend, but for short pilot engagements we can do invoice-only if the vendor accepts an exception.

Pilot details: 3 months, expected spend ~$13,200 (monthly invoices). Attached is the SOW (finovate_sow_v1.pdf). Can Redwood accept an invoice-only path for this pilot while we complete vendor setup? If not, what is the quickest route to get a PO issued from our side?

We can provide the vendor intake form, SOC2 report, and a contact at our procurement team for any questions. Hoping to start billing in early December.

Best,
Sasha Turner
Director, Partner Programs
Finovate Analytics

----
Attachment: finovate_sow_v1.pdf (PDF, 92 KB)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 10:12:03 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Thanks for the SOW and the clear ask, Sasha — excited to run this with your team. Quick summary of Redwood's default practice and the exception path: 

- Default: PO required for new vendor onboarding and recurring billing; this ensures invoice matching and finance controls.
- Exceptions: Small-dollar pilots can be approved on an invoice-only basis if preapproved by Procurement and Finance, with evidence (SOC2, W9, signed SOW) and a completed PO-exception form. The standard temporary threshold we approve without executive signoff is $10k total.

Given your SOW estimate (~$13.2k), we can do one of two pragmatic paths:

Option A — expedited exception (fastest): you submit the vendor intake + evidence and we route a PO-exception form to Procurement. With Priya's approval and Finance signoff we can approve a temporary invoice-only cap up to $12k while contracts are finalized.

Option B — full approval: Procurement and Finance approvals + Director (or above) signoff for a one-off increase to $15k. This takes longer but aligns with your full pilot estimate.

Next steps I suggest:
- Sasha: confirm whether you can reduce the first-month invoice (or accept a 50% prepayment structure) to keep initial billing under $12k to speed start.
- Priya: please confirm what additional evidence you need and an estimated SLA for review.

I'll loop procurement and AM to move this.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference

----
Quoted message below
On Mon, Nov 2, 2026 at 8:47 AM Sasha Turner <sasha.turner@finovate.com> wrote:
> (original message and attachment referenced)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:05:44 -07:00
Subject: RE: Pilot onboarding — PO flexibility request

Thanks Aditya — capturing the evidence checklist and our recommended path below. I ran this past Finance; approximate turnaround times included.

Required evidence for an exception (please upload to shared drive):
- Signed SOW (we have finovate_sow_v1.pdf)
- Vendor intake form / W9 / company registration
- SOC 2 Type II or equivalent security attestation
- Point of contact for billing and for escalation
- Completed PO-exception form (attached stub: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf)

Temporary approval lanes and SLA: 
- Procurement (Priya) can approve up to $12,000 temporary invoice-only carveout with Finance reviewer signoff (24–48 business hours after docs submitted).
- Any request above $12,000 requires Director-level approval (2–5 business days) and additional risk review.

Attachments: redwood_po-exception-form.pdf (fill and return), vendor-risk-worksheet.xlsx (for internal risk scoring).

If Sasha can shave the pilot’s first invoice below $12k (either by shifting month 1 deliverables or by a 50% prepay), we can enable billing and start the pilot while we finish the full approval track. Otherwise we'll route for Director approval — we can fast-track but it will add a few days.

Priya Natarajan
Procurement Manager, Redwood Inference

----

Sasha — quick operational update and next steps. We can approve a temporary invoice-only path capped at $12,000 while we complete the director review for a higher cap. This gets you billing ability immediately for the first invoices.

What we need from you (action items):
1) Complete and return the attached redwood_po-exception-form.pdf (scan or DocuSign)
2) Finalize and sign the SOW (we can send a DocuSign request if helpful) — link to SOW and Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-sow-finovate
3) Provide SOC2 or equivalent security attestation and vendor intake fields in the intake form

Operational notes:
- Send invoices to billing@redwood.com and add the Finovate vendor code FIN-5542 in the invoice reference.
- The temporary approval is valid for 90 days; if your pilot extends beyond that we will need to either issue a PO or re-evaluate the threshold.

Marissa will follow up with a DocuSign link for the SOW and a HubSpot task will be created on the deal (HUB-23457).

Best,
Marissa Cole
